itinerary.title: Arrive Udaipur
itinerary.description: On Arrival at Udaipur, meet our representative and transfer to hotel. Check in at hotel and relax. Evening free for leisure. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay in hotel.

itinerary.title: Udaipur
itinerary.description:
After enjoying breakfast in the hotel, a sightseeing tour of Udaipur including City Palace Complex visit Durbar Hall, Crystal Gallery, Palace Museum and Jagdish Temple. After lunch visit Saheli Ki Bari that is a small ornamental garden built in the honour of Royal Ladies for recreational purpose and visit Lok Kala Mandal & Shilpgram to see the village arts & crafts. In evening enjoy the famous Pichola Lake, Udai Sagar and Fateh Sagar Lake. Return back to hotel for dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.     Udaipur

 What to Buy and Where to Buy:

  Hathi Pol Bazaar: BuyRajasthani miniature paintings, wooden handicrafts for home decor, authentic souvenirs, etc. (Timings:9.30 am to 5.30 pm) Bada Bazaar: Buybandhani and lehariya print sarees, handmade juttis, jewelry made with silver and camel bone (Timings: 9.30 am to 6 pm) Clock Tower: Buypolki, kundan, lac and meenakari jewelry (Timings: 10 am to 8.30 pm) Jagdish Temple Street: Buycamel leather diaries, traditional wooden toys, puppets (Timings: 10 am to 8 pm) Lake Palace Road: Look forbrass and wooden statues of Hindu deities, embroidered textiles, batik and hand printed clothes (Timings: 10 am to 6.30 pm)   

 Must Try Food in Udaipur: On your Udaipur tour, you must enjoy hot samosas at Bohra Ganpati Misthan near Thokar Chauraha (Timings: 8 am to 9 pm); Lala ki Kachori at Asthal Mandir is a must try in Udaipur (Timings: 9 am to 7 pm); Jalebi at Bhole Misthan opposite Delhi gate Police Station (Timings: 7 am to 9 pm)

 Places to Visit in Udaipur on Your Rajasthan Tour:

 Your Udaipur Mount Abu Tour is incomplete without visiting places like City Palace (Timings: 9.30 am to 4.30 pm, open every day, Entry fees: Foreigner/Indian – Rs. 50); City Palace Museum (Timings: 9.30 am to 4.30 pm, open every day, Entry fees: Foreigner/Indian – Rs. 5); Shilpgram Museum (Timings: 11 am to 7 pm, open every day, Entry fees: Foreigner - Rs. 25, Indian – Rs. 15); Manson Palace (Timings: 8 am to 6 pm, open every day, Entry fees: Foreigner - Rs. 80, Indian – Rs. 10); Saheliyon ki Bari (Timings: 9 am to 5 pm, open every day, Entry fees: Foreigner/Indian – Rs. 5); Fateh Sagar Lake, Jaisamand Lake and Rajsamand Lake.

itinerary.title: Udaipur - Mount Abu
itinerary.description:
After an early breakfast drive to the only hill station of Rajasthan Mount Abu. On arrival check into the hotel. After lunch visit to the Dilwara temples constituted of five Jain temples constructed in the beginning of the 11th century with intricate ornamental carvings represents the real art of decoration, followed by the visit to Gomukh temple, Vimala Vasahi, Bhramkumari Temple. Return back to hotel in the evening. Dinner & Overnight at the hotel.    Mount Abu

 Holy Places to See in Mount Abu During Your Udaipur Mount Abu Tour:

  Dilwara Temple: Dilwara temple is a host of five Jain temples.  It took 200 years to build all of them. It is one of the most beautiful pilgrimage spots in the world. It is situated at a distance of 2.5 kms from Mount Abu. (Timings: 12 pm to 5 pm) Gomukh Temple: Gomukh temple is located at a distance of 5 kms from the main town of Mount Abu. You can easily walk till the temple through the markets of the city. The temple is named after a cow. Spring of water flows through the carved mouth of a cow which his considered holy. Vimala Vasahi: This temple forms part of the Dilwara temple. The walls of the temple have beautiful carvings on them. The images of various Jain saints can be noticed. So take some time and visit this historic temple. (Timings: 12 pm to 5 pm) Bhramkumari Spiritual University: It is a place where many practicing yogis visit every year. The place is renowned for giving a peaceful experience through meditation. You must visit this place to rediscover yourself and find the inner joy.   Must See Places in Mount Abu:

  Wildlife Sanctuary: The wildlife sanctuary is located between the Aravalli mountain range. It is spread over an area of 288 square kilometers. The forest has around 250 species of birds and other rare species of wildlife. You must visit here to experience the jungle closely. Nakki Lake: it is one of the most popular tourist attractions in Mount Abu. Nakki Lake is an amazing spot to hang out with friends or family. You can do boating, horse riding, and many other activities. Nakki Lake will give you great memories on your Rajasthan Tour. (Timings: 9.30 am to 6 pm, open all days) Few other places to visit during your Mount Abu tour are Sunset point, Honey Moon point, Guru Shikar and Trevors Tank.   Tips for Rajasthan Holiday Package:

  NRI's must carry their Indian Passport or any other valid documents on the Rajasthan Tour to avail the benefits of local entry fees to various places Foreigners should always carry their passports Visitors of SAARC and BIMSTEC countries shall be charged the fees same as the Indians

itinerary.title: Mount Abu – Udaipur - Delhi
itinerary.description: After breakfast checkout from the hotel on time and drive to Udaipur to catch your flight to Delhi for onward destination. Here trip ends with happy memories.

itinerary.title: Bangalore Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrive at Bangalore’s international airport from where we transfer you to the hotel. Take some rest and begin the city’s sightseeing tour. Options include Bangalore Palace (open 10:00 a.m.-05:30 p.m., fees INR 210 for Indians and INR 450 for foreigners), Tipu Sultan’s Palace (open 08:30 a.m.-05:30 p.m., fees INR 5 for Indians and INR 100 for foreigners), Bull Temple (07:00 a.m.-08:30 p.m.) and Kemp fort Shiva Temple (featuring a 65 ft. tall Shiva Statue). You may like to admire the breathtaking architecture of the government buildings Vidhana Soudha and Vikasa Soudha. Overnight stay at Bangalore.

itinerary.title: Bangalore – Shravanbelagola – Hassan (about 183 km.)
itinerary.description: Start an early morning drive to Hassan, the cradle for the growth of the magnificent Hoysala Temple Architecture. The place has some extremely enchanting religious wonders to its credit. The earlier inconvenient and accident-prone road journey (NH 48 from Bangalore-Hassan) has been recently turned absolutely safe and fast with 4-lane makeover. On the way, stop at Shravanbelagola, the Jain pilgrimage village which is home the world’s largest monolithic statue. The 58 ft. high Lord Gommateshwara statue on Vindhyagiri Hill is a visual treat to the eyes.  Continue the journey and reach the hotel at Hassan. You may visit the fabulous 12th century Chennakesava Temple flaunting the superlative Hoysala Architecture. Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the temple boasts of stunningly decorative panels and turrets and ornate pillars, along with unique carvings and sculptures. Remember to remove shoes and not to shoot the restricted sections. You may also visit the Hoysaleswara Temple (a small fee required for the temple museum) at Halebid, also known for its fascinating architecture and sculptures. Overnight stay at Hassan.

itinerary.title: Hassan – Mysore (about 118 km.)
itinerary.description:
Today, you will visit the royal capital of Wodeyar Dynasty, Mysore, one of India’s cleanest cities and known for spellbinding palaces, temples and gardens. On arrival, pay visit to Chamundi Hills (no plastic zone), the seat to Goddess Chamundeshwari inside a beautiful temple dedicated to her. The 15 ft. high Nandi Bull Statue is a glorious attraction here. Check in at the hotel later.

  Begin the sightseeing tour in the afternoon and visit Mysore Palace (open 10:00 a.m.-05:30 p.m., fee INR 40 for Indians and INR 200 for foreigners, illuminated beautifully on Sundays). Featuring the blend of different architectural styles and surrounded by an elegantly manicured garden, the palace is sure to make you skip a heartbeat or two. You may also visit Sri Jayachamarajendra Art Gallery (open 08:30 a.m.-05:30 p.m., fee INR 20) with fabulous paintings and artifacts on display. In the evening, explore the interesting Devaraja Market with rows of vendors selling fruits, vegetables, flowers and spices. Overnight stay at Mysore.

itinerary.title: Mysore Sightseeing – Srirangapatna
itinerary.description:
Have breakfast before we take you for a small excursion to the river island city of Srirangapatna. It is home to the brilliantly built Ranganathaswamy Temple (dedicated to Lord Vishnu), known for the striking temple towers (gopuras) and other elements with unmatched glory. Few temple areas restrict the entry for foreigners. Later, you may visit Ranganathittu Bird Sanctuary 9open 08:30 a.m.-06:00 p.m., fee INR 50 for Indians and INR 200 for foreigners with additional boating charges), where scores of birds like storks, spoonbills, ibis, kingfishers and darters allure the visitors.

     Get back to Mysore and spend a great time at the imposing Brindavan Gardens (open 10:00 a.m.-09:30 p.m., fee INR 15 for adults and INR 5 for children), known for its musical fountains, amazing greenery, and other highlights. You may also visit other attractions like St. Philomena’s Church and Rail Museum if the time permits. Overnight stay at Mysore.

itinerary.title: Mysore – Bangalore for departure (about 144 km.)
itinerary.description: Travel to Bangalore and transfer to airport or railway station to begin the journey back to home.

itinerary.title: Jabalpur / Umaria /Katni – Bandhavgarh
itinerary.description:
Arrive at the Umaria / Katni Railway Station or Jabalpur Airport (Morning Flight).

 We would arrange vehicle from Jabalpur Airport to Bandhavgarh which is about 210 Kms journey (at an additional cost). If you arrive at Umaria Railway Station or Katni Railway Station, our tour representative shall welcome you and drive you towards Bandhavgarh National Park (transport cost shall be extra and not included in the tour cost).Drive to Bandhavgarh National Park from Umaria is a mere 25 Kms journey while from Katni it is about 140 Kms which takes about 3 hours. 

 Upon arrival ,check-in into luxurious Bagh Sarai resort & take rest. Rest of the day shall be at leisure.

  Dinner & stay overnight at Bagh Sarai resort.

itinerary.title: Bandhavgarh National Park
itinerary.description:
After an early morning tea, we move out for an exciting Safari at Bandhavgarh, the land of tigers.

  Located in the Vindhya Hills, Bandhavgarh is blessed with a large variety of animals e.g. leopards, gaur , chital (spotted deer), Sambar deer , Dholes, nilgais , wild boars, sloth bears, rhesus macaques, black faced langurs, jungle cats, hyenas, porcupines, jackals, foxes, wild dogs, chausinghas and ratels, among others.

 After the safari, we return back to Bagh Sarai resort and have a heart filling lunch. In the afternoon, we leave for another Safari in the park. One gets to see some of the larger specimens of wild boar, Sambhar Deer and Barking Deer found anywhere in the country.

 Later return back to Bagh Sarai resort for a sumptuous dinner.

 Overnight stay at Bagh Sarai,Bandhavgarh.

itinerary.title: Bandhavgarh National Park
itinerary.description:
This day you again venture into Bandhavgarh National Park early in the morning on a tiger trail.Bandhavgarh is credited with being a National park with a high density of tigers per 100 sq. km.This makes the chances of sighting tiger quite bright.

 After the morning safari, you would return back to Bagh Sarai resort to enjoy our lunch.After lunch and relaxing a while, you would embark upon yet another safari into the Bandhavgarh National Park.

 Later, return back to Bagh Sarai resort for dinner and overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Bandhavgarh – Jabalpur / Umaria / Katni
itinerary.description:
In the morning, after breakfast leave for Jabalpur Airport or Umaria / Katni Railway Station well in time for your onward journey with lots of memories & wildlife photographs to cherish for a long long time to come..

 End of tour services..

itinerary.title: Arrive Calicut & transfer to Bandipur
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Calicut railway station, you will be met & received by our representative and will be transferred to your resort in Bandipur National Park. Situated in Mysore District of Karnataka State, Bandipur National Park is home to more than 70 Royal Bengal Tigers, and more than 3,000 Indian elephants. One of the oldest national parks of India, it was among the first nine Tiger Reserves created in India at the launch of Project Tiger in 1973. Arrive Bandipur and check into your resort. Rest of the day is at leisure to enjoy the calm serenity of the forest resort. Stay Overnight at Bandipur.

itinerary.title: Bandipur National Park
itinerary.description: Today we will have two safaris in the Jungle to spot the wildlife and to explore the Bandipur National Park which was once a private hunting reserve for the Maharaja of the Kingdom of Mysore. In the morning, we enjoy our first game drive in the jungle for bird watching and tiger tracking. Bandipur also houses Leopard, Asiatic Wild Dogs, Striped Hyenas, Jackals, Jungle Cats, Leopard Cats, Rusty Spotted Cats, Palm Civet, Small Indian Civets, Otters, Gaur, Barking Deer, Mouse Deer, Four-Horn Antelope, Pangolin, Indian Porcupine, Fruit Bat, Spender Loris, Bonnet Macaque, Wild Pig and variety of reptiles such as Rock Python, Cobra, Marsh Crocodile, Monitor Lizard, Rat Snake, Wolf Snake, Common Krait and Green Keel Back are also found here. Later, we return to the resort for breakfast and lunch. In the afternoon, we leave for another safari for wildlife exploration in Bandipur National Park. Stay Overnight  at Bandipir.

itinerary.title: Bandipur National Park – Calicut
itinerary.description: Early morning, we will have our last Safari / Game Drive into the Bandipur National Park. If time allows, we can also visit Venugopala Swamy Temple at Himavad Gopala Swamy Betta. It is a hill located at a height of 1450m which is the highest peak in the Bandipur National Park.  Return to the hotel & have breakfast before you drive back to Calicut railway station to board the train for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Goa
itinerary.description: Welcome the Hub of beaches – Goa. On arrival, at Goa Airport/Railway station, meet and transfer to the beautiful Resort/ hotel of your choice. Check into the Resort and relax since the day free at Leisure. Enjoy dinner &Overnight stay at Resort.

itinerary.title: Goa
itinerary.description: In this morning enjoy your breakfast, Day free for Beach/Resort activities till lunch. After Lunch, proceed for half day city tour covering St.BascillaBom Jesus, Se Cathedral, Miramar Beach, and Cruise in Mandovi River. Dinner and Overnight stay at Resort.

itinerary.title: Goa
itinerary.description: After Breakfast, proceed for half day tour covering Fort Aguada, Anjuna Beach and Vagator Beach. Back to resort for hot Lunch. In after, after Lunch visit Mangesh Temple, Colva Beach. Enjoy your dinner in beautiful evening &Overnight stay at Resort.

itinerary.title: Goa
itinerary.description: In the morning after Breakfast in the resort,Thisday is free for your own activities and leisure’s. Like you can go for a long ride of Goa City by hiring Motor bike drive by your own. In the evening enjoy the shopping and go for a candle light dinner etc. and back to resort till late evening. Overnight stay at Resort.

itinerary.title: Goa- Departure
itinerary.description: After breakfast transfer toAirport / Railway Station for your onward destination with happy memories.

itinerary.title: Trivandrum – Kovalam (20 Km / appx. 30 min.)
itinerary.description: Welcome to Kerala, popularly known as God’s own Country!!  As you arrive at Trivandrum airport or railway station, our arranged vehicle would take you to your hotel in Kovalam. Check in at the hotel and get refreshed.   Located on the Arabian Sea, Kovalam is one of the most sought after beach destination in India.  Rest of time free for relaxing. Alternatively, you can go for a stroll on nearby beach.  Stay at Kovalam.

itinerary.title: Sightseeing of Kovalam
itinerary.description: Today after breakfast, you would be visiting famous beaches in Kovalam like Hawa beach, Ashoka beach, Light House beach etc. you can also enjoy various water sports activities on the beaches.  Back to your hotel. Stay at Kovalam.

itinerary.title: Kovalam – Kanyakumari (82 Km / appx. 2 hrs.)
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, proceed to Kanyakumari, the southern most tip of mainland of India.

  Kanyakumari is located on the confluence of Indian ocean Arabian sea and Bay of Bengal that offers picturesque views to visitors.

  Upon arrival, check in at the hotel. After getting refreshed, visit Kumari Mata Mandir and Vivekananda rock memorial. Enjoy spectacular views of the sunset. Back to your hotel. Stay at Kanyakumari.

itinerary.title: Kanyakumari – Varkala (140 Km / appx. 3 hrs.)
itinerary.description:
Early in the morning, you can view the sunrise.

  After breakfast, proceed to Varkala, another beautiful beach town in Kerala.

  Upon arrival at Varkala, check in at the hotel.

  Later visit to famous Varkala beach. Stay at Varkala.

itinerary.title: Varkala
itinerary.description: This day is at leisure to relax or to enjoy Varkala beach and activities on the beach. You can also see local fishermen doing their daily routine. Stay at Varkala.

itinerary.title: Varkala – Trivandrum (65 Km / appx. 1 hr. and 30 min.)
itinerary.description:
It’s time to say goodbye to beautiful Kerala.

  After breakfast, transfer to Trivandrum airport or railway station as per your onward connection. End of a magnificent tour!!

itinerary.title: Kochi – Munnar (130 km/ appx. 3 1/2-4 hrs.)
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Kochi airport or Ernakulam station, proceed to Munnar. A scenic journey through hills and mountains takes you to hill Munnar, one of the most famous hill stations in South India.  Once you arrive in Munnar, check in at the hotel. Later you can explore this beautiful town or take rest in your hotel.   Stay at Munnar.

itinerary.title: Sightseeing of Munnar
itinerary.description: After taking breakfast at the hotel, go for tour of Munnar.   In this tour, visit Mattupettydam, various tourist points in Munnar, Devikulam and tea estates.  Stay at Munnar.

itinerary.title: Munnar – Thekkady (120 km/ appx. 3hrs.)
itinerary.description: After taking your breakfast, proceed to Thekkady.   Thekkady is known for Periyar wildlife sanctuary and spice plantations.  Check in at the hotel and quickly get refreshed. Later proceed for sightseeing tour of Thekkady.   Visit spice plantations around and other tourist places. You can also have a nature walk.   Stay at Thekkady.

itinerary.title: Periyar wildlife sanctuary /Thekkady
itinerary.description: At 6 am, proceed to visit Periyar wildlife sanctuary. Take a boat ride in lake Periyar. You can see variety of animals and birds during this enjoyable boat ride.  Later in the day, explore this scenic region of Thekkady. You can have various nature walks, jungle trails etc.  Stay at Thekkady.

itinerary.title: Thekkady – Kumarakom (120 Km/ appx. 2 1/2-3 hrs)
itinerary.description: After breakfast and proceed to Kumarakom.  Once you arrive at Kumarakom, check in at the hotel. Later proceed for sightseeing tour. Visit  Kumarakom bird sanctuary, house boat jetty and tiny villages located on the banks of backwaters.  Stay at Kumarakom.

itinerary.title: Kumarakom – Alleppey   (52 Km / appx. 1 hr.)
itinerary.description: After having breakfast, at 10.30 am, proceed to Alleppey for your houseboat stay.  Upon arrival, check in at the houseboat.  Enjoy serenity of backwaters as houseboat sails through. You can also see villages and scenic islands in the backwaters.  Stay on the houseboat is really an unique experience. While enjoying the backwaters, you would have delicious cuisines on board prepared and served fresh!!  Stay on houseboat.

itinerary.title: Alleppey – Kochi (64 km/ appx. 2 hrs.)
itinerary.description: It’s time to say good bye to vibrant Kerala.  After having breakfast, transfer to Kochi airport or Ernakulam railway station, as per your train/flight connection.  End of wonderful and memorable tour!!

itinerary.title: Arrive Leh
itinerary.description: On arrival at Leh, meet with our local assistance & then transfer to the hotel. Check in the hotel and relax. In the late afternoon short walk in the City of local market, visit to Shanti Stupa & Sankar Gompa. Dinner & Overnight at the hotel.

itinerary.title: Leh
itinerary.description: After finishing your breakfast proceed for sightseeing including Shey Palace, Thiksey Monastery & Stok Palace Museum. After a hot lunch in the hotel, visit and Hall of Fame, Gurudwara Pathar Sahib, Magnetic Point and drive back to hotel, en route visit Spituk Monastery. Enjoy dinner and overnight at hotel.

itinerary.title: Leh - Khardung La - Leh
itinerary.description: After enjoying breakfast in the hotel drive towards Khardung-La (90 kms round trip), which is the highest motorable pass in the world (alt: 18,380 ft.). On the drive while coming back, enjoy the views of the Indus Valley and the endless peaks of the Zanskar range. Rest of the day free at your own leisure. Dinner & overnight at hotel.

itinerary.title: Leh Departure
itinerary.description: After breakfast on time transfer to the airport to catch your flight for back home.

title: Bird Watching in Jim Corbett Park

itinerary.title: Delhi - Jim Corbett National Park [280 kms/06-07 hrs]
itinerary.description: In the early morning drive to Jim Corbett National Park. On arrival, check into the wildlife resort. Evening is free to explore the surrounding of Jim Corbett National Park. Enjoy dinner and overnight stay at resort.

itinerary.title: Jim Corbett National Park
itinerary.description: Early morning visit the forest area including the grasslands to watch the birds. We need to cover some area of trekking along the Ram Ganga River because it is very honouring for some kind of birds such as water fowl, mergansers, waders, river lapwings. Return back to Resort. Dinner & Overnight stay at the wildlife resort.

itinerary.title: Jim Corbett National Park
itinerary.description: After breakfast we will include the Elephant Safari for birds watching. Elephant safari will helps us to give the best possible and clear view of birds watching. This day we can watch some other species of birds like Puff-throatd Babbler, White rumped Shama, Lammergeier etc. Later return back to Resort. Enjoy bonfire followed by the dinner & Overnight stay at wildlife resort.

itinerary.title: Jim Corbett National Park - Panghot [50 kms/2 hrs]
itinerary.description: After an early breakfast drive to Panghot. Panghot is situated in the district Naintal. It is a small village area but excellent place to birds watching. You can find here more than 100 kinds of species of birds. In this pleasant area you can find the range of Himalayan birds species as Blue Winged Minla, Khalij Pheasant, Woodpecker, Lammergeier etc. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay at Panghot.

itinerary.title: Panghot [Nainital Hills]
itinerary.description: After breakfast proceed for full day sightseeing area for birding such as Naina Peak, Kilbury hills etc. and return back to Lodge. Dinner & Overnight stay at wildlife Lodge.

itinerary.title: Panghot - Sat Tal [45 kms]
itinerary.description: After an early breakfast at wildlife lodge drive to Sat Tal. Birding around Sat Tal. This day we will cover the wild area of Naukuchiya Tal, Bhimtal and forested area of Nainital hills. Return back to resort for dinner & Overnight stay at Sat Tal.

itinerary.title: Sat Tal - Mukteshwar [50 kms]
itinerary.description: After an early refreshment start journey to Mukteshwar. Enroute you can watch various kind of Himalayan birds species. Evening time is free to explore the natural beauty of Mukteshwar. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay at Resort.

itinerary.title: Mukteshwar - Binsar (80 kms)
itinerary.description: In this morning starts your journey to Binsar wild life sanctuary. From here you can enjoy the excellent view of Himalaya there. You can see the majestic view of snow capped Himalayan ranges peaks, such as Trishul, Panchachuli, Nanda Devi, Nanda Kot, etc. Overnight stay at the Jungle Resort at Binser.

itinerary.title: Binsar
itinerary.description: After enjoying breakfast proceed for full day birds watching in the forest area of Binsar wild life sanctuary. Return back to resort for dinner & Overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Binsar - Delhi [380 kms/11 hrs]
itinerary.description: After early breakfast in the resort drive back to Delhi. On arrival, transfer to Airport/Hotel for your onward destination.

itinerary.title: Delhi
itinerary.description: In the morning pickup from your convenient place in Delhi and proceed for full day sightseeing of Delhi including Laxmi Narayan Temple, Akshardham Temple, Lotus Temple, India Gate, Parliament House, Jama Masjid, Red Fort, Qutub Minar, and Humanyun’s Tomb. Later in the evening on time drive to Neemrana. On arrival, check into the hotel. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay in Neemrana.

itinerary.title: Neemrana
itinerary.description: After a leisurely breakfast in the hotel visit to Neemrana Fort - A 14th century hill fort ruled by Chauhans till 1947 (Prthviraj Chauhan). Now the fort has been converted to heritage hotel which is located very close to Kesroli. Return back to hotel in the evening for dinner & Overnight stay at hotel in Neemrana.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: After a healthy breakfast check out from the hotel and drive back to Delhi. On arrival enjoy shopping or other independent activities in Delhi. Later, transfer to your convenient places for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Delhi
itinerary.description: Welcome on Arrival in Delhi, meet and assist by tour our representative & transfer to your hotel. Overnight stay at the hotel.

itinerary.title: Delhi
itinerary.description: After a healthy breakfast in the hotel proceed for full day sightseeing of Old & New Delhi including Laxmi Narayan Temple, Akshardham Temple, Lotus Temple, India Gate, Parliament House, Jama Masjid, Red Fort, Qutub Minar, and Humayun's Tomb. Return back to hotel in the evening for dinner & overnight stay the hotel.

itinerary.title: Delhi - Manali [521 Kms/12-13 hrs]
itinerary.description: Start early morning drive from Delhi to Manali by Car. On arrival at Manali, check into the hotel. The evening is free at leisure. Enjoy your dinner and Overnight stay at Manali.

itinerary.title: Manali
itinerary.description: After breakfast proceeds for full day city tour including Hadimba Devi Temple, Manu Temple, Tibetan Monastery and Vashisht Village. The afternoon is free at leisure. In the evening explore the local market of Manali. Enjoy dinner and Overnight stay at a hotel.

itinerary.title: Manali
itinerary.description: After enjoying breakfast in the hotel full day excursion visiting Kothi Gorge, Gulaba, Marhi, Rohtang Pass which is the most visited tourist spot in Himalaya having snow round the year (In case if it is not closed due to snow fall), Solang Valley and return back to a hotel. Dinner and Overnight stay at a hotel.

itinerary.title: Manali - Kullu [Kullu festival]
itinerary.description: After having breakfast this morning, drive to Kullu & full day visit to Dussehra festival. This Hindu festival is celebrated all over India and Dussehra in Kullu is famous all over the country. Return back to a hotel in the evening for dinner & Overnight stay at the hotel in Manali.

itinerary.title: Manali - Delhi
itinerary.description: After an early breakfast in the hotel, drive back to Delhi. On arrival at Delhi, transfer to Airport/Railway Station to board the flight/train for next destination.

itinerary.title: Arrival at Delhi
itinerary.description: Gangaur Festival Jaipur has its first stop at Delhi where you arrive by rail or air and are escorted to the booked hotel in the city. After checking-in and relaxing for few hours, you are free to spend the rest of the day at your will. It would be a great idea to visit the bustling Chandni Chowk(open till 08:00 p.m.; closed every Sunday; best transport modes include metro and cycle-rickshaw) market area for shopping and to relish a variety of street snacks and Indian sweets. Stay overnight at Delhi.

itinerary.title: Delhi Sightseeing
itinerary.description:
Have to breakfast and join us to visit the hotspots of Delhi. Ruled by different dynasties over thousands of years, the city has distinctive historical and cultural charm which is evident from brilliant architectural masterpieces. Consider it as the opportunity to watch the historic Old Delhi meeting the contemporary New Delhi. Start with Laxmi Narayan Temple (also called Birla Temple; open 04:30 a.m. to 09:00 p.m.) which is constructed in Nagara Architecture style and boasts of attractive carvings, fountains, and gardens. Next, visit Akshardham Temple (09:30 a.m. to 06:30 p.m.; closed every Monday) which has a stately architecture and attractions like lush green gardens and musical fountains. 

 The next highlight of Jaipur Gangaur Festival tour the incredible Lotus Temple (09:00 a.m. to 05:30 p.m.; Monday closed; permission required for photography) which is designed in the form of lotus petals using white marble. From there, we head towards India Gate, the monumental arch commemorating the bravery of Indian soldiers. Drive past SansadBhawan (Parliament House) which boasts of stupendous modern architecture.Later, visit Jama Masjid (open 07:00 a.m. to 06:30 p.m.; INR 200 for photography), an extremely peaceful mosque with supreme architecture. 

 Next, you would visit the colossal Red Fort (09:30 a.m. to 04:30 p.m.; closed on Monday; entry fee INR 35/500 for Indians/foreigners), the red sandstone wonder with many great structures. Then, visit QutubMinar (07:00 a.m. to 05:00 p.m.; entry fee INR 30/500 for Indians/foreigners), one of the tallest minarets in the world. Humayun’s Tomb (07:00 a.m. to 06:00 p.m.; entry fee INR 30/500 for Indians/foreigners; INR 25 for video cameras), an enchanting structure amidst sprawling gardens is the final attraction of the day. Return to the hotel and stay overnight.

itinerary.title: Delhi – Agra (233 km.)
itinerary.description:
Finish your breakfast and start the road trip to Agra. The erstwhile capital of the grand Mughal Empire, the city has a fascinating profile boasting of enthralling Mughal heritage. On arrival, visit the first attraction of the day – Sikandra (open 06:00 a.m. to 06:00 p.m.; entry fee INR 15/115 for Indians, BIMSTEC, and SAARC nationals/others), the tomb of great Mughal Emperor Akbar. Geometric and floral designs on red sandstone and marble make it a spellbinding structure. 

 One of the most exciting moments of Gangaur Festival Jaipur arrive as you visit the resplendent TajMahal (open 06:00 a.m. to 06:00 p.m.; entry fee INR 40/530/1000 for Indians/BIMSTEC and SAARC nationals/others). This white marble beauty needs no introduction as you must have read much about its brilliant architecture and the love story of Shah Jahan and MumtazMahal which inspired its construction. Take time to explore the structure before checking in at the booked hotel. 

 Relax and have lunch before we visit a marble factory in the city. Watch expert artists engaged in crafting irresistible marble carvings and performing majestic inlay work. From there, visit Agra Fort (open 06:00 a.m. to 06:00 p.m.; entry fee INR40/80/550 for Indians/BIMSTEC and SAARC nationals/others), the magnificent red sandstone structure boasting of royal palaces, pavilions, thrones and more.Stay overnight at Agra.

itinerary.title: Agra – Jaipur (244 km.)
itinerary.description: Start from Agra after breakfast as we drive to the next destination – Jaipur. On the way, you would be excited to stop at FatehpurSikri(open 09:00 a.m. to 05:00 p.m.; entry fee INR 40/40/510 for Indians/BIMSTEC and SAARC nationals/others)which is yet another addition of Mughals to the splendid Indian heritage wealth. Explore the marvelously designed red sandstone and marble structures like BulandDarwaza, PanchMahal, Diwan-i-Aam, Diwan-i-Khas, Tomb of SalimChishti and much more. Resume the road trip to Jaipur and check in at the booked hotel on arrival. Have dinner and stay overnight.

itinerary.title: Jaipur Sightseeing
itinerary.description:
Before you actually attendGangaur Festival Jaipur celebrations, spend this day visiting the world-famous attractions of the city. Jaipur, apart from being a state capital, is the most visited city to experience Rajasthani folk culture, cuisines, artworks and festivities. After breakfast, we take you for an exhilarating elephant ride to ascend Amber Fort located on a hilltop (08:00 a.m. to 06:00 p.m.; entry fee INR 25/200 for Indians/foreigners and INR 10/100 for Indian/foreign students, ride costsINR 900 for two). Watch in awe the remarkable views from the top and explore royal structures. 

 Later, visitHawaMahal (09:00 a.m. to 05:00 p.m.; entry fee INR 50/200 for Indians/foreigners) which boasts of a honeycomb-like design with hundreds of jharokhas (small windows). Then, visit the majestic City Palace (09:30 a.m. to 05:00 p.m.; entry fee INR 25/35/150 for children/Indians/foreigners; INR 50/150 for still/video camera) andJantarMantar Astronomical Observatory (09:30 a.m. to 04:30 p.m.; entry fee INR 15/50/200 for students/Indians/foreigners; INR 50/100 for still/video camera).

 Reserve evening for a visit to ChokhiDhani, the traditional village-like restaurant which serves platters of scrumptious Rajasthani cuisines. You would be thoroughly entertained by the performances like folk dances, acrobatics and puppet shows and activities like bullock cart ride. Stay overnight at Jaipur.

itinerary.title: Gangaur Festival Jaipur
itinerary.description:
Finally, the day arrives when you would be a part and parcel of the festival. During Gangaur celebrations, young unmarried girls observe fast wishing for a suitable match while married women do so for long lives of their husbands and marital bliss. Celebrations begin a day after Holi in the month of March or April. 

   Here are some great aspects of the festival many of which you would enjoy during Gangaur festival tour Jaipur:

  Women prepare idols of Shiva and Gauri using clay and painting them in beautiful colours. Idols are also decorated with pretty clothes and jewellery items. Women apply mehndi (myrtle paste) in some truly remarkable designs on their palms and feet. Women carrying earthen pots (called Ghudlias, with a number of holes and earthen lamps inside) on their heads to various homes, collecting jiggery, ghee, sweets and cash as blessings. Women dressed up in attractive traditional clothes and jewellery and singing folk songs. Ghewar, the tasty Indian sweet is just not worth missing. Processions and departure of Gana and Gauri as women immerse their idols in water on the concluding day.  Enjoy the entire day observing these highlights. Stay overnight at Jaipur.

itinerary.title: Jaipur – Delhi (273 km.) – Departure
itinerary.description: Leave Jaipur after breakfast and drive back to Delhi. As the Gangaur Festival Jaipur tour comes to end, we drop you at the airport or railway station.

itinerary.title: Arrive Rajkot & transfer to Gir National Park
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Rajkot airport, you will be met & received by our representative and will be transferred to your hotel in Gir National Park. Last home of the Asiatic Lion, Gir National Park is a dry deciduous habitat for the lions, dominated by short and gnarled teak trees, thorn bushes and grassland. Within the Sanctuary, there are several human settlements of cattle herders, the Madharis, with an extensive count of livestock, all of who co-exist harmoniously with the animals of the wild. Arrive Gir, check into your hotel and relax for rest of the day. Evening is free at leisure to explore the local area. Stay Overnight at Gir National Park.

itinerary.title: Gir National Park
itinerary.description: Early morning, Jeep Safari into the Gir Forest National Park, well known as the remaining habitat of the Asiatic Lion. Gir is also known as the place to see leopards, spotted deer (chital), peafowl and raptors. After the safari, we return for breakfast at the hotel. After lunch in the afternoon, we get ready for another thrilling Game Drive / Jeep Safari into the Gir Forest National Park. Some of the other mammals in the forest are nilgai, sambar, chousinga, wild boar, ruddy mongoose, langurs, jungle cats, striped hyenas, Indian porcupine and chinkara. Stay Overnight  at Gir National Park.

itinerary.title: Gir National Park – Rajkot Airport
itinerary.description: Early morning, we will have our last Jeep Safari / Game Drive into the Gir Forest National Park. Return to the hotel & have breakfast before you drive back to Rajkot Airport to board the flight for your onward journey.

title: Golden Triangle and Khajuraho Dance Festival Tour

itinerary.title: Arrival Delhi
itinerary.description: Welcome on arrival at Delhi meets and assists by our representative, then move towards to hotel. Check into the hotel and Overnight stay in Delhi.

itinerary.title: Delhi - Jhansi [By Train Depart at 06:15 hrs, Arrival at 10:48 hrs.] Jhansi - Khajuraho [By Road 180 Kms/ 04 hrs]
itinerary.description: Early morning pick up from your hotel & transfer to Railway station to board on the Shatabdi train to Jhansi. Tea & breakfast will be served in the train. On arrival at Jhansi, meet with our representative & drive to Khajuraho. En-route visit Orchha – It is a historical site with magnificient temples and monuments. In the afternoon, a continue drive to Khajuraho. On arrival at Khajuraho, check into the hotel. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay at hotel in Khajuraho.

itinerary.title: Khajuraho
itinerary.description: This morning after breakfast in the hotel proceed for full day sightseeing of Khajuraho visit Eastern & Western Group of Temple’s. In the evening enjoy Khajuraho festival of dances. It draws the best classical dancers in the country who perform against the spectacular backdrop of the floodlit temples. You will see the different classical Indian styles are represented - Kathak, Bharatnatyam, Kuchipudi, Odissi, Manipuri, and many more. Later, return back to hotel for dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Khajuraho
itinerary.description: After having a healthy breakfast this morning, a full day excursion to Raneh falls. Enjoy Khajuraho Festival of Dances in the evening. Return back to hotel for dinner & Overnight stay in Khajuraho.

itinerary.title: Khajuraho - Jhansi - Agra [By Car & then train Depart at 17:56 hrs, Arrival at 20:25 hrs]
itinerary.description: After enjoying breakfast in the hotel, visit to the local markets of Khajuraho. In the afternoon in time transfer to Jhansi Railway Station to board the train to Agra. On arrival at Agra, meet our representative & transfer to hotel. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay at hotel in Agra.

itinerary.title: Agra
itinerary.description: After leisure breakfast in the hotel proceeds for full day sightseeing tour of Agra covering Taj Mahal, Agra Fort & Sikandra Fort. In the afternoon visit the marble factory to see the beautiful inlay of precious and semi-precious stones in the marble amazing intricate craftsmanship, you can do the shopping also. Return back to hotel in the evening for dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Agra - Fatehpur Sikri - Jaipur [235 Kms/05 hrs.]
itinerary.description: After an early breakfast drive to Jaipur. We also witness en-route one of the magnificent places, the Fatehpur Sikri, Jama Masjid, the Buland Darwaza and Palace of Jodha Bai also remain on the traveling charts. Birbal Bhawan & Panch Mahal also makes you mesmerized throughout the tour. On the arrival at Jaipur, check into the hotel. Dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Jaipur - Delhi [By train Departs at 1745 hrs, Arrival at 22:30 hrs]
itinerary.description: After an early breakfast checkout from the hotel and proceed for full day city tour of Jaipur. Enjoy elephant ride at Amber Fort. Later, explore the attractions of Jaipur as like Hawa Mahal, City Palace, Jantar Mantar, and the list is endless. Visit the colossal forts and ambrosial palaces - a perfect blend of Indian, Mughal and of course European architecture that make the perfect travel attractions in Rajasthan. Also enjoy the shopping at local bustling bazaars of the city. In the evening in time, transfer to Railway Station to board the train to Delhi, Dinner will be served in the train. On reaching at Delhi, meet with our representative & transfer to hotel. Overnight stay in Delhi.

itinerary.title: Delhi - Departure
itinerary.description: After a leisure breakfast in the hotel proceed for full day Sightseeing tour of Old & New Delhi covering Red fort, Jama Masjid, Raj Ghat, Humayun's Tomb, Qutab Minar, India Gate, Parliament house etc. Enjoy shopping in the evening and on time transfer to Airport/ Railway station for your onward destination.

itinerary.title: Arrival at Delhi
itinerary.description:
On arrival in Delhi, our representative would welcome you with highest standards of hospitality. You will be then escorted to the pre-booked hotel of your choice. 

 Check into the hotel and you will get enough chance to freshen up, afterwards proceed for sightseeing tour of New Delhi covering all the prominent places such as the Red Fort, India Gate, Jama Masjid, Lotus Temple, and Laxmi Narayan Temple. 

 You also get a chance to witness the Parliament House, Rajpath, and Rashtrapati Bhawan.

  Overnight stay at the hotel is planned.

itinerary.title: Delhi -Agra [225 kms/ 4 hrs&30 mins approx.]
itinerary.description:
Early morning proceed towards Agra trip. En-route visit Fatehpur Sikri as well, where you would witness the largest gateway, Buland Darwaza and the Jama Masjid and the tomb of famous Sufi saint Shaikh Salim Chisti.

 Later continue your drive to Agra.Upon arrival at Agra, check-in into the hotel and relax. 

 After lunch, visit the world famous Taj Mahal, Agra Fort & Sikandra Fort.

Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Agra - Jaipur [232 kms/ 5 hrs approx.]
itinerary.description:
After breakfast drive to Jaipur. Upon arrival at Jaipur, check-in into the hotel and freshen up. 

 Later proceed on tour to the exquisite forts and palaces of Jaipur visiting the Amber Fort,City Palace, Hawa Mahal and Jantar Mantar etc.

  In Evening visit the Choki Dhani village resort, enjoy the cultural dance and the Rajasthani dinner in the resort. 

 Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Jaipur - Ranthambore [180 kms/ 3 hrs & 30 mins approx.]
itinerary.description: After breakfast proceed for Ranthambore. We also make you go to Sawai Madhopur. Here you check into a forest lodge and witness the widespread beauty on the outskirts of the Ranthambore National Park. After enjoying your lunch, you enjoy an exhilarating jungle safarion jeep or on elephants in Ranthambore.Overnight stay at the lodge.

itinerary.title: Ranthambore - Delhi [440 kms/ 8 hrs approx.]
itinerary.description: In the morning, after enjoying our breakfast, we head towards to spot the Indian tigers, which remain elusive. We also visit the Ranthambore Fort, which is situated inside the tiger reserve. Thereafter we check-out the lodge thus moving towards Delhi in the afternoon. Thenmake you transfer to the Delhi International Airport to board the flight for your onward destination.

itinerary.title: Kochi to Munnar (Distance & time taken 135 Kms/4.5Hrs)
itinerary.description: Arrive at Kochi Airport/Railway Station where our representative shall meet you and assist you.Leave for Munnar,the misty mountain hill station.Reach Munnar by evening.Check-in into the hotel.Savour the sumptuous dinner at the hotel.Overnight stay at the hotel.

itinerary.title: Munnar Sightseeing
itinerary.description: Leave for full day sightseeing tour of beautiful mountainous environs of Munnar.Visit Eravikulam National Park,famous for endangered species of Nilgiri Tahr.Later also visit Mattupetty Dam which has a scenic agricultural garden.You can also take a motorized boat to ride through the dam.Reach back hotel by evening.After your dinner,relax in the hotel.Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Munnarto Thekaddy(Distance & time taken 112 Kms/3.5Hrs)
itinerary.description: After a healthy and sumptuous breakfast leave Munnar by 8:30 am am to reach Thekaddy by afternoon.Check into the hotel.Relax in the hotel or visit the local market to buy some souvenirs  or tea or essential oils.Later in the evening visit the Kalari Theatre to view a performance of Kathakalli. Reach back hotel.Enjoy a candle light dinner.Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Thekaddyto Alleppey(Distance & time taken 165 kms/4Hrs)
itinerary.description: et up early in the morning and leave hotel by 5 am for a boat ride in Periyar National Park.After boat ride reach back hotel.Have your breakfast and leave for Alleppey also known as Alappuzha by 9 am.Reach Allappuzha and check-in  into resort.Relax for a while and later go for a shikara ride in pristine waters of Vembanad lake, witnessing the  Venice of South India where the villages are lined up on the shores of backwaters of Kerala.Later return back to the hotel.Overnight stay at the hotel

itinerary.title: Alleppey to Kovalam(Distance & time taken 166 Kms/4 Hrs)
itinerary.description: After morning breakfast leave for Kovalam.Reach Kovalam by afternoon.Check-in into the hotel.You can also visit Poovar Island to see the beautiful golden beach which are washed away by Arabian sea on oneside and backwaters on the other.Later in the afternoon, visit the beach to view beautiful sunset at Kovalam.Reach back hotel and relax.Overnight stay at Hotel.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Kanyakumari(One way Distance & time 92 Kms/3 Hrs)
itinerary.description: After your breakfast in the hotel leave for a day trip of Kanyakumari,the southernmost tip of India.While being in Kanyakumari you can visit Kumari Amman Temple which is a Shakti Peeth where as per the mythology Goddess Parvathi did severe penance to seek Lord Shiva’s hand in marriage.Other notable places to visit are statue of Tamil saint Thiruvalluvar,which stands 133 feet high and Vivekanand Rock Memorial.Later return back to Hotel.Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Kovalam-Trivandrum Airport/Railway Station
itinerary.description: After morning breakfast check out of the hotel and proceed to Trivendrum airport/railway station for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrival at Jaisalmer
itinerary.description:
Arrive at Jaisalmer and begin the tour with our representative who will receive you and escort you to the hotel.
 Take some time to relax and freshen up for your first tryst with the exciting heritage.
 You will be visiting the grand yellow-sandstone Jaisalmer Fort, deservingly called the Golden Fort for shinning in various shades of gold throughout the day. Explore the beautiful palaces and temples that are admired for their rich carvings and excellent architecture.
 Reach the festival venue in the evening and witness some extremely entertaining folk and cultural performances. Take part in some fun-filled competitions before you get back to the hotel.

itinerary.title: Jaisalmer Sightseeing - Visit Sand Dunes
itinerary.description:
Finish the breakfast and get ready for another exciting visit to the venue of Jaisalmer desert festival.
 Get amazed to witness the fascinating camel races, folk performances, sports events, art displays and more.
 Later, visit the royal mansions called Salim Singh Ji ki Haveli and Nathmal Ji ki Haveli that would leave you speechless with their grandeur.
 Begin the trip to Sam Sand Dunes at 03:00 p.m. Savour the remarkable sunset views and enjoy thrilling camel safari before enjoying dinner amidst heart-warming settings.
 Get back to the hotel.

itinerary.title: Departure from Jaisalmer
itinerary.description:
Spend the morning at leisure.

 Some great options include Gadsisar Lake and Tazia Tower. You may also prefer to pay another visit to the festival.

 Alternatively, you may plan shopping at the local handicraft bazaars. Later, you will be transferred to the railway station to board the scheduled train.

 The tour would end here.

itinerary.title: New Delhi
itinerary.description: Arrive at Delhi airport/ railway station, meet with our representative and get transferred to hotel. Night stay in Delhi.

itinerary.title: New Delhi to Jodhpur (By flight)
itinerary.description:
Morning after breakfast check out from Hotel and transfer to Airport. Fly from Delhi to Jodhpur. 

 Reach  Jodhpur,  the Blue City of Rajasthan where our representative shall greet you and transfer you to a hotel. 

 Get fresh and proceed for the local sightseeing of Jodhpur. Visit Mehrangarh Fort which sits majestically atop a hill, Mahamandir Templ, and the Kailana Lake. 

 Night stay in Jodhpur.

   Jodhpur

 Before we proceed to the Desert Festival Jaisalmer tour, we will stay just 1 day in Jodhpur, for visiting the most important places here.

  Mahendragarh Fort: This magnificent fort situated on the top of a hill is just 5 km from Jodhpur. It remains open from 9 am to 5 pm. Entry fee for Indians is Rs 60 while for foreigner, it is Rs 500. Indians have to pay Rs. 150 for audio guide while foreigners have to pay Rs 400 for it. Video camera charge is Rs. 200. Jaswant Thada: This wonderful mausoleum opens at 9 am and closes at 5 pm. Children and senior citizens have no entry fee. For Indians, entry fee is Rs 15 and that of foreigners is Rs 30. Still camera fee is Rs 30 and video camera fee is Rs 50. Umaid Bhawan Palace Museum: The enchantingly beautiful palace cum museum remains open from 9 am to 5 pm and you will need at least 2 hours to visit it. Entry fee for the Indians is Rs 15 and that of foreigners is Rs 50. Balsamand Lake: It has no entry fee and it remains open all day and night. Keep at least 2 hours in hand for witnessing the splendid beauty of this place. Mandore Gardens: Tourists can visit this place between 8 am and 8 pm and its entry fee is Rs. 50 per person.

itinerary.title: Jodhpur to Jaisalmer ( By road ) (271 Kms/ 6 Hrs )
itinerary.description:
After a sumptuous  breakfast, proceed for Jaisalmer by road.

 By late afternoon reach  Sam sand dunes and check in into  Desert Camp.Later go for an enjoyable camel ride at Sam sand dunes.Also enjoy the breathtaking view of the sunset at Sam sand dunes. After camel ride comes back to the swiss tent in the desert camp and in the evening enjoy dinner with Rajasthani folk music & dance. 

 Night stay in Sand Dunes.

   Jaisalmer

 It will take around 5-6 hours to reach Jaisalmer from Jodhpur by car depending on the road condition and traffic.

  Sam Sand Dunes: Desert festival Jaisalmer tour package includes 1 night stay in same Sane Dunes. Ideal time to visit these dunes in the morning from 4 am to 6 am and in the afternoon from 4 pm to 7 pm. Enjoy the camel safaris, Rajasthani folk music and dances, yummy Rajasthani foods and stay either in the mud cottages or in the Swiss tents. There are different desert camps here and you need to pay between 4000 INR and 10000 INR per night depending on the camp you choose.  Desert Festival Jaisalmer: Things You Must Know

 1. Jaisalmer desert festival will be held from February 8th to February 10th in 2017. The festival starts with the Shobha Yatra or the Ceremonial Procession from the Jaisalmer Fort and it proceeds to Shahid Poonam Singh Stadium by 11 am and after that the Desert Festival Jaisalmer is inaugurated.
2. Dresses: People wear traditional colorful dresses and describe the calamities and accomplishments of the desert through their dances and songs.
3. Major Highlights: The Gair and the fire dance are the main attractions. Gymnastic acts are shown by a group of local wanderers who are known as the Kalabaz or the Nat's group.  Emotional folk music recitals as well as ballads are done by the Bhopa's Langhas and Manganiars.
4. Competitions held during the Jaisalmer desert festival: Camel polo between Border Security Force and polo club, dancing in different camel formations, camel races, and gymnastics on the back of the camels. After these competitions are over, Camel Tattoo competition by the BSF is held. Other competitions include turban tying, the longest mustache, Miss Moomal, tug of war and Mr. Desert or Maru-Shri. Pitcher race or Matka race is also held.
5. End of the festival: The Desert festival Jaisalmer ends with the Rajasthani folk concert by the Manganiyar and Langa musicians and Kalbelia dancers, cricket match display by Air Force and parachuting. It is a ritual to end the festival on a full moon day having Jaisalmer Fort in the background.
6. Other attractions: Enjoy yummy food, numerous stalls having Rajasthani handicrafts, souvenirs and artifacts, puppet shows, acrobatic shows.
You need not buy tickets to view the warmth and grandeur of this festival. It is organized by the Rajasthan Tourism Development Corporation in order to attract more tourists.

itinerary.title: Jaisalmer Desert Festival
itinerary.description:
After your morning breakfast ,leave for Jaisalmer town to enjoy the Desert Festival.

 During the desert festival Jaisalmer witness the Shobha Yatra (Ceremonial Procession ) which proceeds to Shahid Poonam Singh Stadium at around 11:05 AM followed by the formal inauguration of Desert Festival .

 Also enjoy the  Moomal - Mahendra Competition, Moustache Competition, Turban Tying Competition, Miss Moomal & Mr. Desert Competition and many such thrilling events. 

 Overnight stay at hotel  in Jaisalmer.

itinerary.title: Jaisalmer Desert Festival
itinerary.description:
After your morning breakfast spend the day enjoying the desert festival where you would witness the Camel Decoration Competition, Shan-E-Marudhara Competition, Tug of War between Indian hosts & their foreigner guests, Pitcher Race(Matka Race), Camel Polo Match between Polo Club Jaisalmer and Border Security Force(BSF).

 Later in the evening  enjoy the soulful folk music & dance by renowned folk artists of Rajasthan which include performances by the Manganiyar and Langa musicians and dance by Kalbelia dancers also.

 Overnight stay in Jaisalmer.

itinerary.title: Jaisalmer – Jodhpur / transfer to New Delhi by flight
itinerary.description:
Early morning after breakfast check out from a hotel at around 06.00am and leave for Jodhpur by road to catch the flight for New Delhi. 

 Arrival at Delhi Airport and transfer to Hotel.Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Delhi
itinerary.description: After your morning breakfast check out from Hotel. Transfer to airport for onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Ramnagar & transfer to Corbett National Park
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Ramnagar railway station, you will be met & received by our representative and will be transferred to your resort in Corbett National Park. Situated in the foothills of the Himalayas, Corbett National Park is one of India's most beautiful wildlife areas having the population of tiger around 160, which makes this park the most important bastion of this endangered species in India. Spreading across an area of 1288 sq km with the core areas of 521 sq km, the zone of Corbett Tiger Reserve is filled with variant flora and fauna species for an amazing wildlife experience. Arrive Corbett National Park and check into your resort. Rest of the day is at leisure to enjoy the calm serenity of the forest resort. Stay Overnight at Corbett.

itinerary.title: Corbett National Park
itinerary.description: Corbett Park’s virgin forest and jungle are home to numerous species of fauna. The rich biodiversity of the park is a perfect place for bird enthusiasts; the area is considered as one of the best bird-watching areas in India, with some 600 species having been sighted in the park. Today we will have two safaris in the Jungle to spot the wildlife and to explore the Corbett National Park. With tigers and elephants ruling this area, Corbett Reserve is the area where the abundance of wildlife can be found with the majority of finest species like Leopard, Himalayan Gorals, wild Boars, Langurs, Indian pangolins, Porcupine, Sambhar & Chital, Barking, otters, ghoral (goat-antelopes), Hog Deer and more. Some of the birds commonly seen here are Osprey, Black Winged Kite, Shikra, Crested Serpent Eagle, Pallas' Fishing Eagle, Spotted Eagle, Babblers, Minivets and Shrikes etc. Later, we return to the resort for breakfast and lunch. In the afternoon, we leave for another safari for wildlife exploration in Corbett National Park. Stay Overnight  at Corbett.

itinerary.title: Corbett National Park – Ramnagar
itinerary.description: Early morning, we will have our last Safari / Game Drive into the Mudumalai National Park. Later transfer to Calicut railway station to board the train for your onward journey.

title: Jim Corbett Park Weekend Tour Package

itinerary.title: Day 01: [Friday Evening] Delhi - Corbett [280 kms/ 06-07 Hrs]
itinerary.description: In evening pickup you from the hotel/ home and transfer to railway station to catch your train to Ramnagar. On arrival at early morning start your journey to Corbett National Park, transfer to the wildlife resort booked for you, Welcome drink and a hot lunch will be served at the Resort. Our representative will introduce you with the wildlife guide and driver, they will be with you during your trip. In evening time you can go to enjoy some adventurous activities such as rock climbing, rappelling, river crossing many more in the back our river Kosi. Even you can go to visit the famous Maa Garjiya temple also. Rest of the time is free for leisure at your own. Enjoy dinner and overnight stay at resort.

itinerary.title: Day 02: Corbett - Delhi
itinerary.description: Early morning, you will get a wakeup call to get ready to enjoy wildlife safari in Corbett National Park. The wildlife safari will be start from 6:00 am to 9:00 am. On this time, you have several occasions to find the wildlife animals very close and explore the unbeatable flora and fauna of Corbett Tiger Reserve.   Later return back at resort for breakfast and Check out from the resort on time to catch your train to Delhi. On arrival transfer to your hotel/ home for onward destination. Here wildlife trip ends.

itinerary.title: Arrive at Govindghat (1828 m.)
itinerary.description: Kagbhushudi Lake trek begins atGovindghat, which is at the height of 1,828 m.Govindghat is located in Chamoli district of Uttarakhand. This place is a base for many camp sites. To reach Govindghat, Jolly Grant Airport in Dehradun is the closest airport situated at a distance of approximately 292 km. Rishikesh railway station is the closest railway junction which is about 273 km. away. Govindghatis well-connected by road as well. You can come from Joshimath (via NH 58) which is 22 km. away.Once you reach Govindghat, get in touch with our team and they will guide you towards your arranged accommodation for an overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Trek from Govind ghatto Bhyundar Village to RoopDhungi (1828 m., 12 km.)
itinerary.description: Have breakfast and start trekking towards Bhyundar village, the starting point of the trek to Kagbhushudi Lake. The total distance that we are going to cover today is 12 km. The trek path is little longer but easier in gradient. Though there is steep climbing for the first hour of the trek, but later it is an easy trek. While trekking from Bhyundar village to RoopDhungi, you come across rocky areas, glaciers, shrubs, rivers, etc. Trekkers have to walk on boulders and glacial moraines till they reach the Lake. Enjoy the sounds of gushing water of River Alaknanda, while you move forward. Trek from Bhyundar village passes through dense bear-infested forests. During the trekking route, you get to see Rhododendron forest and Haathi Parvat. After a long day, have overnight stay in a makeshift tent at RoopDhungi.

itinerary.title: Trek from Roop Dhungi to Simartoli to Rajkharak (14 km.)
itinerary.description: Post breakfast, we move from RoopDhungi to Simartoli, and later to Rajkharak. The total distance that we are going to cover today is 14 km. The entire path is beautiful and soothing to eyes. The journey allows you to get enchanted by the majestic view of colorful flowers all across the path and snow clad mountains in the background. While on the route, we pass by many small areas like Kargila, Simartoli, Bankbara, Dang khark, Rajkhark, etc. They are beautiful campsites for trekkers. A lot of shepherds use this area as pastureland to graze their livestock. After the tiring day, take rest in a makeshift tent at Rajkharak.

itinerary.title: Trek from Rajkharak to Kagbhushandi (5300 m., 8 km.)
itinerary.description:
Your wait is finally over as we reach day 4, the most exciting day of Kagbhushudi Lake trek. Today we head from Rajkharak to Kagbhushandi (5300 m.) which is an 8 km. patch. The initial path to the lake is through green valley and meadows, later followed by climbing the boulders. However, the instant thought of the mesmerisinglake will keep you going through all the tough routes. 

 Trekkers come across wildlife especially bears while trekking to Kagbhushandi. On the way, you come across beautiful views of Neelkanth (6596 m.), Hathi Peak (6727 m.), Ghodi Parvat (6590 m.), Chaukhamba and Nar-Narayan Peaks. There is awide variety of Himalayan flora and fauna in the valley. You can witness Brahma kamal, Myriad, Fen kamal and many more flowers. Once you reach the lake, you will be awestruck by the beauty of nature. The sacred lake is 500 m. long and 250 m. wide. It is one of the high altitude lakes in the Himalayan region. It is surrounded by huge snow-cladpeaks, which enhances its beauty several times. During the month of July to September, you can see the entire lake is decorated with wild, colorful and high altitude flowers. After having a great day, your overnight stay is in a makeshift tent at Kagbhushandi.

itinerary.title: Trek from Kagbhusundi to Gounchha Udiyar (8 km.)
itinerary.description: On day 5, we continue the trek from Kagbhusundi to Gounchha Udiyar. The entire 8 km. stretch is covered by sprawling green meadows, gushing streams, lofty panoramic view and alpine forest. So don’t forget to take pictures here and preserve the lovely memories of the trek. This trek is a visual treat to the eyes. On the route, you can witness the lifestyle of tribal villagers who are living a nomadic life here. Get to learn their culture, their lifestyle, have a meal with them to know more about the place in the true sense. Take rest after a hectic day at a makeshift tent in Gounchha Udiyar.

itinerary.title: Trek fromGouncha Udiyar to Painka to Govindghat (10 km. trek and 7 km. Drive)
itinerary.description: Have breakfast and get ready for Gouncha Udiyar to Painka trek. After reaching Painka, we head towards Govindghat. We are going to cover this distance with 10 km. trek and 7 km. drive. Painka is a small and beautiful village located in the lap of Garhwal Himalaya. The trek passes through rocky and narrow path along withdense forests. Trekkers also come across the spring coming from Barmal Peak. The beauty of this place will leave you astonished and you would want to visit here again and again. The blissful walk ends on reaching Govindghat. Check in at the guest house arranged by us. Enjoy hot dinner and relish the entire evening.

itinerary.title: Govindghat to Haridwar/ Rishikesh/ Dehradun
itinerary.description: Kagbhushudi Lake trek ends here. Have delicious breakfast and later checkout from the guest house. Proceed for your onward journey to Haridwar / Rishikesh / Dehradun.

itinerary.title: Arrival Jabalpur & transfer to Kanha (150 kms/3-4 hrs)
itinerary.description: You will be met on your arrival at Jabalpur airport /railway station and transferred to the resort in Kanha National Park (150 kms/3-4 hrs). Located in the Mandla district of Madhya Pradesh, Kanha National Park covers nearly 2,000 sq. km. of undulating country. During the transfer, we will pass through green belt of reserved forests, small villages and townships. The Surpan River meanders through Kanha's central maidans-grasslands. These grasslands are interspersed with forests of sal, teeming with varieties of deer (the barasingha), chital (spotted deer), chousingha, nilgai, the gaur (Indian bison) and wild pig, is 'tiger country'. Upon arrival at Kanha National Park, we check into the resort and enjoy wilderness or rural life style spread around resort. Rest of the day is free at leisure or to explore the local area. Stay overnight at Kanha.

itinerary.title: Kanha National Park
itinerary.description: Today we will have 02 safaris, morning & afternoon game drives in the park. We will wake-up early in the morning and get ready for our first game drive in the park. Kanha is the home to 'hard ground' barasingha (cervus duvauceli branderi) the only surviving population of this Central Indian sub-species. In addition to tigers there is lot more to see in Kanha safari like rare Swamp Deers, Sambar, Spotted Deer, Indian Gaur, Wild Dog and shy Leopards etc. This area is also ideal for spotting the dhole or wild dog. Langurs, wild boar, water fowl and birds are also commonly seen. Later, we return to the resort for breakfast and lunch. In the afternoon, we leave for our second safari for wildlife exploration in Kanha National Park. Stay overnight at Kanha.

itinerary.title: Transfer to Jabalpur (150 kms/3-4 hrs)
itinerary.description: In the morning, we will have our last safari / game drive in Kanha Tiger Reserve. After the safari, return back & after having breakfast at the resort, leave for Jabalpur city & transfer to the airport / railway station for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Chennai Arrival – Trichy (324 km.)
itinerary.description: Arrive at the international airport of Chennai, the capital of Tamil Nadu, and transfer to the hotel with our representative. After a brief stay, travel to the city of Trichy, known for its sculpted temples, and check in at the hotel on arrival. After some rest, visit the first temple of the tour – the Rock-cut temple located within the Rock Fort Complex. The entire complex located on the billions of years old rock and overlooking River Cauvery is extremely spectacular in terms of architecture.   Later, visit Jambukeswarar Temple of Thiruvanaikaval near the city. The temple, which marks the powerful devotion of an elephant and a spider towards Lord Shiva, is definitely an architectural wonder. Huge precincts, marvellous sculptures, towering gopurams and grand temple cars are among the highlights. The Shiva Lingam below the holy Jambu Tree represents the lord’s presence as water, one of the five elements of life. After this fascinating temple trip, get back to the hotel.

itinerary.title: Trichy – Rameshwaram (265 km.)
itinerary.description: Have breakfast and visit the island of Rameshwaram. Check in at the hotel on arrival and relax. Today, you will explore Ramanathaswamy Temple (one of the four Char Dham destinations) and other temples in the vicinity. Also, admire the engineering marvel of Pamban Rail Bridge connecting the island to the mainland India before returning to the hotel.

itinerary.title: Rameshwaram – Kanyakumari (300 km.)
itinerary.description: Proceed to your next destination, Kanyakumari, after breakfast. Check in at the hotel on arrival at Kanyakumari, the southernmost destination of mainland India and located on the grand confluence of Arabian Sea, Indian Ocean and Bay of Bengal. After some rest, you will visit Kanyakumari Temple and Vivekananda Rock Memorial. Enjoy the magnificent sunset view before retiring to the hotel.

itinerary.title: Kanyakumari – Madurai (240 km.)
itinerary.description: Bid farewell to Kanyakumari after breakfast and move to the next destination – Madurai, one of the oldest inhabited cities of the world. Check in at the hotel on arrival and take some rest. Start the city tour with a visit to the spectacular Meenakshi Amman Temple. You will be left speechless by the enthralling temple complex, one of the largest in the world, with 14 soaring gateway towers, more than 33,000 sculptures, vimanas with gold sculptures and shrines dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi (Parvati) and Sundareswarar (Shiva). Explore the wonders like the Golden Lotud Temple Tank (blessed to contain no marine life) and the Hall of Thousand Pillars, known for exquisite carvings.   Later, visit the nearby Thirumalai Nayakar Mahal, known for splendid courtyards and dance hall boasting of musical pillars. From here, you will visit the Mangamma Palace which houses one of the five Gandhi Memorial Museums of India. Discover the priceless collections of letters, manuscripts, photos, paintings and original art efacts associated with the Father of the Nation. Return to the hotel at the end of this exciting day.

itinerary.title: Madurai – Thiruvannamalai (340 km.)
itinerary.description: Leave Madurai after breakfast and travel to Thiruvannamalai. After checking in at the hotel, relax and start the sightseeing trip. Visit Arunachaleswara Temple and other attractions like Gingee Fort, Padaveedu and Sathanur Dam before returning to the hotel.

itinerary.title: Arrive Guwahati & transfer to Kaziranga
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Guwahati airport, you will be met & received by our representative and will be transferred to your hotel in Kaziranga National Park. In the heart of Assam, covering an area of 430 sq-kms, Kaziranga National Park is inhabited by the world's largest population of one-horned rhinoceroses, as well as many mammals, including tigers, elephants, panthers and bears, and thousands of birds. In 1985, it was declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO for its unique natural environment. Arrive Kaziranga, check into your hotel and relax for rest of the day. Evening free at leisure to explore the local area. Stay Overnight  at Kaziranga.

itinerary.title: Kaziranga National Park
itinerary.description: After an early wake up, you will be taken to the riding point at Western range of the national park for an Elephant Ride. The main attraction of it is one-horned Rhinoceros with wild Buffalo, Tiger, Elephant, variety of Deer, Python and variety of migratory birds, water birds, predators, scavengers and game birds. We return to the hotel for breakfast and refreshment. Later we will go for local sightseeing which includes famous plain tea gardens of Assam, Karbi Ethnic village and local model village. After the visits, we return to the hotel for Lunch. In the afternoon, we leave for jeep safari at central range of the national park. Enjoy rest of the evening at hotel or resort at leisure. Stay Overnight at Kaziranga.

itinerary.title: Kaziranga National Park
itinerary.description: Kaziranga National Park, located along the banks of the River Brahmaputra in Assam, is one of the popular wildlife destinations in India. Today, we will have 02 more safaris in the park and the opportunity to see the animals. Kaziranga has a wonderfully large expanse of land covered with tall elephant grass, marshlands and swamps along with a wide plain cover, that make it slightly easier to spot the animals while in this forest.

itinerary.title: Kaziranga National Park – Guwahati Airport
itinerary.description: After having breakfast at the hotel, you will drive back to Guwahati & will be dropped at the airport for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Bharatpur & transfer to the hotel
itinerary.description: You will be met on your arrival at Bharatpur railway station and will be transferred to the hotel. Arguably the best water bird sanctuary in India (Some would say in the world), Keoladeo National Park is the World Heritage site & is the perfect home to almost thousands of birds from across the world. Nesting indigenous water birds and migratory water birds specifically the Siberian Crane, the Bharatpur Sanctuary also boasts with many species of Sambar, Chital, Nilgai and Boar. Amazingly, at least 230 species of birds have made this sanctuary their favorite home and is the most favorable and protected zone for them. Upon arrival, check into the hotel & relax for rest of the day. Stay overnight at Bharatpur.

itinerary.title: Keoladeo National Park
itinerary.description: Today we will have 02 safaris in the park. Also known as Bharatpur Sanctuary, it is one of the oldest wild reserves in India and was created 250 years ago when it was named after the Keoladeo (Shiva) temple within its boundaries. The park is also referred as Ghana National Park since 'Ghana' means dense referring to the thick forest that covers the whole Bharatpur area. The numerous counts of the bird species are the major attractions for the tourists in Bharatpur. These species fly through distant places like Siberia and Central Asia specifically in winters. Migratory birds at Bharatpur Bird Sanctuary include several species of Cranes, Pelicans, Geese, Ducks, Eagles, Hawks, Shanks, Stints, Wagtails, Warblers, Wheatears, Flycatchers, Buntings, Larks and Pipits, etc. Along with that some major counts of Sambal, Chital, Nilgai and Boar can also be found at the sanctuary. Stay overnight at Bharatpur.

itinerary.title: Transfer to Bharatpur Railway Station
itinerary.description: After having breakfast at the hotel, you will be transferred to the railway station to board the train for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Kochi
itinerary.description: Welcome to Kerala, God’s own Country!!  As you arrive at Kochi airport or Ernakulam station, our arranged vehicle would take you to your hotel. Check in at the hotel and get refreshed.   Later you would be going for Kochi city tour.  Kochi is located on Arabian sea and it is one of the most important and busy ports in India.  This vibrant town has many things to offer, history, culture, cuisines and shopping!!  In city tour of Kochi, you would be visiting Jewish Synagogue (not open on Saturdays and Jewish holidays) in Jew Town,  Mattancherry Palace (not open on Fridays and public holidays). Later visit Chinese Fishing Nets, the only ones in India and St. Francis Church (not open for visitors on Sundays from 08.00 to 11.00 hrs.) the oldest European Church in India.   In the evening, you can go for shopping. Kochi is famous for spices, silk and South Indian sarees and sweets also!!  To see the artistic side of the town and to experience a good time, go for Kathakali dance performance at 5 pm. Stay at Kochi.

itinerary.title: Kochi – Munnar (130 km/ appx. 31/2-4 hrs.)
itinerary.description: Proceed to Munnar after breakfast. A journey through hills and mountains takes you to hill Munnar, one of the most famous hill stations in South India.  Once you arrive in Munnar, check in at the hotel. Later you can explore this beautiful town or take rest in your hotel.  Stay at Munnar.

itinerary.title: Sightseeing of Munnar
itinerary.description: After taking breakfast at the hotel, go for tour of Munnar.   In this tour, visit Mattupetty dam, various tourist points in Munnar, Devikulam and tea estates. Stay at Munnar.

itinerary.title: Munnar – Thekkady (120 km/ appx. 3 hrs.)
itinerary.description: After taking your breakfast, proceed to Thekkady.   Thekkady is known for Periyar wildlife sanctuary and spice plantations.  Check in at the hotel and quickly get refreshed. Later proceed for sightseeing tour of Thekkady.   Visit spice plantations around and other tourist places. You can also have a nature walk.  Stay at Thekkady.

itinerary.title: Periyar wildlife sanctuary /Thekkady – Allppey (142 Km/ appx. 2 1/2-3 hrs)
itinerary.description: At 6 am, proceed to visit Periyar wildlife sanctuary. Take a boat ride in lake Periyar. You can see variety of animals and birds during this ennjoyable boat ride.  Later come back to the hotel, have your breakfast and proceed to Alleppey. Upon arrival, check in at the houseboat.  Enjoy serenity of backwaters as houseboat sails through. You can also see villages and scenic islands in the backwaters.  Stay on the houseboat is really an unique experience. While enjoying the backwaters, you would have delicious cuisines on board prepared and served fresh!!  Stay at houseboat.

itinerary.title: Alleppey– Kovalam (170 Km / appx. 4 hrs.)
itinerary.description: After having breakfast, at 10.30 am, proceed to Kovalam, one of the most popular beaches in India.     Upon arrival, check in at the hotel.  After you get refreshed, visit various beaches in and around Kovalam. The famous beaches are Hawa beach, Light House beach and Ashoka beach. You can also enjoy water sports activities on the beaches.    Stay at Kovalam.

itinerary.title: Kovalam– Trivandrum – Kovalam (20 Km/ appx. 1 hr. one way)
itinerary.description: After breakfast, at 10.30 am, go for city tour on Trivandrum, capital city of Kerala.  Visit famous Padmanabhaswami temple(dhoti for gentlemen and saree for ladies is mandatory to wear). Later visit Palace museum and Raja Ravi Varma Art Gallery.  Back to Kovalam. In rest of the time,you can roam around on beach in Kovalam.  Stay at Kovalam.

itinerary.title: Kovalam – Kanyakumari (82 Km / appx. 2 hrs.)
itinerary.description: After breakfast, proceed to Kanyakumari, the southernmost tip of mainland of India.  Kanyakumari is located on the confluence of Indian ocean Arabian sea and Bay of Bengal that offers picturesque views to visitors.  Upon arrival,check in at the hotel. After getting refreshed, visit Kumari Mata Mandir and Vivekananda rock memorial. Enjoy spectacular views of the sunset. Back to your hotel. Stay at Kanyakumari.

itinerary.title: Kanyakumari – Trivandrum (85 Km / appx. 2 hrs.)
itinerary.description: It’s time to say goodbye to land of mountains, backwaters and beaches.  After breakfast,transfer to Trivandrum airport or railway station as per your onward connection. End of a magnificent tour!!

itinerary.title: Arrive Delhi
itinerary.description: On Arrival at Delhi, you will be meeting by our representative & then transfer to hotel. Check in at hotel & Overnight stay in Delhi.

itinerary.title: Delhi
itinerary.description: After enjoying breakfast this morning proceed for full day city tour of entire Delhi which covers all the prominent destinations including Laxmi Narayan Temple, Akshardham Temple, Lotus Temple, India Gate, Parliament House, Jama Masjid, Red Fort, Qutub Minar, and Humanyun’s Tomb. Return back to hotel in the evening for Overnight stay the hotel.

itinerary.title: Delhi - Bhubaneswar [By flight]
itinerary.description: After a healthy breakfast in the hotel, on time transfer to Airport to connect the flight to Bhubaneswar. On arrival, meet with our representative & transfer to hotel. Check in and relax for some while. In the afternoon a half day city tour of Bhubaneswar visiting Parasurameswara Temple, Dhauli, Lingraj Temple and return back to hotel for dinner &  Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Bhubaneswar
itinerary.description: After having breakfast this morning, proceed for full day excursion to Buddhist triangle of Udaigiri, Ratnagiri and Lalitgiri. Return back to hotel in the evening. Dinner & Overnight stay at hotel in Bhubaneswar.

itinerary.title: Bhubaneswar - Puri [65 Kms /02 hrs]
itinerary.description: In the morning after breakfast, drive to Puri. On the way, visit Pipili, and Raghurajpur. On arrival at Puri, check into the hotel. After lunch, free to relax on beach at Puri. Dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.
