itinerary.title: Puri - Konarak - Puri [An Excursion to famous Konark Festival]
itinerary.description: After a leisure breakfast in the hotel, visit the famous Jagannath Temple (Non-Hindus are not allowed) and also visit to Loknath Temple. In the afternoon drive to Konark to see the Konark Festival. In Konark, the Sun Temple is famous as a world heritage site.  This venue is famous for festival of classical dance and music which is held annually in the month of December. Here dancers from all over the country comes and perform in the open air auditorium. The festival you will find the Odissi, Bharathnatyam, Manipuri, Kathak and Chau Dance. You can also find a Crafts Mela here, with a different variety of handicrafts and tasty cuisine during the festival. Later return back to hotel in the late evening for overnight stay in Puri.

itinerary.title: Puri - Bhubaneswar - Departure
itinerary.description: This morning after breakfast in the hotel, drive to Bhubaneswar to connect the flight back to Delhi. On reaching at Delhi Airport, meeting & assistance followed by transfer to International Airport/Railway station to board the flight/train for your onward destination.

itinerary.title: Arrival Leh
itinerary.description: Arrive Leh airport and transfer to hotel. On arrival, check in at hotel. In the evening time short walk around Leh bazaar. Dinner & Overnight stay in Leh.

itinerary.title: Leh
itinerary.description: After breakfast proceed for full day excursion to the famous monasteries, it is interesting to visit early in the morning to enjoy the praying ceremony at Thiksey monastery and continue drive to Hemis monastery which is one of the most famous monastery of the Ladakh region. While returning back to Leh visit to Shey Palace. Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Leh - Alchi - Uletokpo [75 Kms]
itinerary.description: After an early breakfast  drive to Uletokpo camping which is one of the most beautiful camping in the Ladakh region on the way visit Spituk monastery which is just 5 kms from Leh town. Alchi monastery is the only monastery in the Ladakh on flat ground. In afternoon time reach Uletokpo. In the evening time enjoy sitting on the bank of river coming down from the Ridzong side. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay at Camp.

itinerary.title: Uietokpo - Lamayuru - Leh [180 Kms]
itinerary.description: After breakfast on time continue drive to Lamayuru via Khaltsi. From here drive 22 kms to reach Lamayuru. It the oldest and spectacularly set Lamayuru monastery founded in the 10th Century. Visit the monastery. After lunch drive back to Leh which will take about 4-5 hrs drive, on the way back stop at magnetic hill. Dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Leh - Khardongla Pass – Leh [85 Kms]
itinerary.description: Enjoy breakfast and proceed for full day excursion to Khardongla pass which is the highest motorable pass in the word 5600 Mtrs and has been recorded in the Gunnies Book of world records. At the top enjoy the nature beauty of snow capped mountain. In the evening back to hotel for dinner & Overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Leh - Pangong Lake – Leh [290 Kms]
itinerary.description: Start early morning drive to Pangong Lake. En-route enjoy the beautiful panoramic view of the villages below the pass. After crossing the pass halt for picnic, lunch at Tsoltak. Arrive Durbuk and from there a short drive to the western shore of the lake. One can enjoy landscape in the back drop of the lake. This famous blue brackish Lake of Pangong is 5-6 Kms wid and over 144 Kms long with half of its running the other side of the INDO CHINA BORDER. The scenery of this beauty is unforgettable. In the late afternoon drive back to Leh. Dinner & Overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Departure - Leh - Delhi
itinerary.description: After breakfast transfer in time to the airport to connect onward destination flight.

itinerary.title: Leh Arrival
itinerary.description: To begin the tour, you will land at the Leh Airport and meet our representative. With his assistance, transfer to the booked hotel. Spend good amount of time to get used to the high-altitude sickness before opting for an evening walk through the traditional bazaars of the town. Dinner will be served at the hotel.

itinerary.title: Leh - Monastery Sightseeing
itinerary.description: Today, you will be visiting the architecturally and culturally beautiful monasteries in and around Leh. Discover the simple yet invigorating lifestyle of the monks and the charming Buddhist paintings at Thiksey and Hemis Monasteries that enjoy the unspoiled natural beauty full of mountains, valleys and rivers. Later, you will visit the incredible Shey Palace which boasts of outstanding architecture and a museum which houses priceless Buddhist artefacts. Get back to the hotel at Leh and enjoy the dinner.

itinerary.title: Leh - Pangong Lake - Leh (320 km.)
itinerary.description: Start an early morning drive to the mesmerizing Pangong Lake after having breakfast. Drive past the world’s third highest mountain pass road of Changla Pass (about 17,350 ft. high). Have the pleasure of exploring unique and flawless vistas of nature throughout the journey. Reach the lake which rests at the feet of beautiful mountains and has lush green pastures nearby. Explore the amazing avifauna and fauna near the lake and get astounded by the overwhelming peace. Watch speechlessly as the nature changes its colours and drive back to Leh, enjoying the scenic route once again. Enjoy dinner on arrival.

itinerary.title: Leh - Nubra Valley - Leh (240 km.)
itinerary.description: Start another exciting day with breakfast and get ready for some more surprises on your way. Drive through the world’s highest motorable road (about 18,380 ft. high), enjoying the remarkable views of snow-clad mountains and rivers to reach the captivating Nubra Valley. Discover small villages amidst greenery and visit some popular monasteries in the region before returning to Leh.

itinerary.title: Leh – Sham
itinerary.description: Have breakfast before we take you to another fascinating region of Ladakh. Spend time at the top attractions like Pathar Sahib Gurudwara and Hall of Fame Museum. Be a spectator to the heart-warming confluence of River Indus with River Zanskar. Get back to Leh by evening and have dinner at the hotel.

itinerary.title: Leh Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to the airport early morning and board the scheduled flight to end the tour.

itinerary.title: Arrive Guwahati & transfer to Manas
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Guwahati airport, our representative will receive you at the airport and will transfer you to the hotel in Manas. Situated in Kamrup-Golapara district, south east of river Manas, Manas National Park is known for its Project Tigers, Rhinos & Elephants, and is Assam's one of the two Tiger Reserve. The scenic beauty and rare wealth of wild life combine with this unique world heritage site offer one of the most enthralling experiences. Migratory birds come here during winter and fly back at the onset of summer. Along the river banks one can spot water birds such as mergansers, Brahminy ducks and egrets. Stay Overnight at Manas.

itinerary.title: Manas National Park
itinerary.description: Today, we will go on 02 jungle safaris which will take us to the interiors of the wildlife haven. An incredible chance to witness many wild species; among which common are elephants, deers, leopards and if lucky, an occasional tiger. Early morning, we proceed for an Elephant Ride in Manas National Park. Later we return to the hotel for breakfast. Post breakfast, we proceed for a Jeep Safari to Mathanguri - Uchila Road. The road cuts through woodland cover and savannah and grassland patches. Sighting of animals in Manas is not as good as in Kaziranga but the diversity of the habitat is very high. We return to the hotel and rest of the day is free to go for short hikes for bird-watching, and get to interact with the tribal people who live sustainably around the national park. Stay Overnight at Manas.

itinerary.title: Manas National Park
itinerary.description: The park covers an area of 2,840 kms, but its core area is 360 km. Today, we will have 01 more safaris in the park and the opportunity to see the animals. If you wish, we can also visit one of the nearby Villages to see the culture and tradition of the Bodo Tribe. Stay Overnight at Manas.

itinerary.title: Manas National Park – Guwahati Airport
itinerary.description: After having breakfast at the hotel, you will drive back to Guwahati & will be dropped at the airport for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Calicut & transfer to Mudumalai
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Calicut railway station, you will be met & received by our representative and will be transferred to your resort in Mudumalai National Park. Mudumalai is in the north west side of the Nilgiri hills of Tamilnadu & is one of best sanctuaries with thickly wooded hills, plateaus, deep valleys, waterfalls, rivers, marshes and streams. The area is also being termed as Madumalai Tiger Reserve due to the presence of around 48 tigers in the Nilgiri Reserve across which tigers are free to roam. In April 2007, the Tamil Nadu state government declared Mudumalai as a Tiger Reserve. Arrive Mudumalai and check into your resort. Rest of the day is at leisure to enjoy the calm serenity of the forest resort. Stay Overnight at Mudumalai.

itinerary.title: Mudumalai National Park
itinerary.description: Mudumalai Wildlife Sanctuary is located at the foot of the Nilgiri hills or Blue Mountains in Tamil Nadu. The sanctuary is mainly an elephant preserve, with over 600 pachyderms here. Mudumalai is actually made up of a number of hunting preserves - Kargudi, Theppakkadu and Masinagudi. Today we will have two safaris in the Jungle to spot the wildlife and to explore the Mudumalai National Park. We will get to see jackals on the hunt for smaller animals & different packs of dholes or wild dogs are operational in Mudumalai, largely preying on deer. Chital, sambar, gaur, tiger, panther, dhole, sloth bear, wild boar, Jungle cats, porcupine, mongoose and civet are other residents. Later, we return to the resort for breakfast and lunch. In the afternoon, we leave for another safari for wildlife exploration in Mudumalai National Park. Stay Overnight  at Mudumalai.

itinerary.title: Mudumalai National Park – Calicut
itinerary.description: Early morning, we will have our last Safari / Game Drive into the Mudumalai National Park. Later transfer to Calicut railway station to board the train for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Calicut & transfer to Nagarhole
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Calicut, our representative will receive you at the railway station and will transfer you to the resort in Nagarhole. Nagarhole National Park, referred as Rajiv Gandhi National Park, is a popular tiger & elephant reserve of India, and houses more than 270 species of birds. Designated as National Park in the year 1988, the forest is one of the popular destinations for jungle activities & is a protected forest that is known mostly for preserving Tigers and Elephants species. Arrive Nagarhole, check into your hotel and enjoy rest of the time at leisure in the resort. Stay Overnight at Nagarhole.

itinerary.title: Nagarhole National Park
itinerary.description: Nagarhole forest was given its name after the Sanskrit word Naga which means Snake which is a reference used to the snake-structured streams that flow through here. Today, we will enjoy 02 wildlife safaris in the jungle to get closely acquainted with the flora and fauna of Nagarhole. With increasing popularity of Nagarhole National Park each day, UNESCO is considering to include it in World Heritage Sites. During the safaris, one can spot animal and bird species like tiger, leopard, Indian bison or gaur, sambar, red giant flying squirrel, spotted deer or chital, spotted deer or chital, bamboo pit viper, common wolf snake  Malabar grey hornbill, red headed vulture, Indian scimitar babbler etc. Stay overnight at Nagarhole.

itinerary.title: Nagarhole National Park – Calicut
itinerary.description: Early morning, we will have our last Safari / Game Drive into the Nagarhole National Park. Return to the hotel & have breakfast before you drive back to Calicut railway station to board the train for your onward journey.Early morning, we will have our last Safari / Game Drive into the Nagarhole National Park. Return to the hotel & have breakfast before you drive back to Calicut railway station to board the train for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Guwahati & transfer to Nameri
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Guwahati airport, our representative will receive you at the airport and will transfer you to the hotel in Nameri. Home to a large number of elephants, Nameri National Park has a wide variety of rare reptiles such as the Assam roof turtle Kachuga sylhetensis or the Khasi hill terrapin, once believed to be extinct and rediscovered at Nameri. The leopard is another predator that lays its claim to the plentiful prey in the form of sambar, hog deer, bison, muntjac, wild boar, capped langur and the Malayan giant squirrel. The sloth bear, Himalayan black bear and the highly endangered hispid hare are also found here. Arrive Nameri, check into your hotel and relax for rest of the day. Evening free at leisure to explore the local area. Stay Overnight at Nameri.

itinerary.title: Nameri National Park
itinerary.description: Today, we will go for a Jungle safari in Nameri National Park. Travelling through the Nameri National Park is normally on elephant back or by taking boat rides on the Jia Bhoroli river. Situated at the foothills of the Eastern Himalayas, on the banks of the splendid Jia Bhoroli river, Nameri is a patchwork of forest and wetlands. Against a hilly backdrop, across the snow-filled waters of the Jia Bhoroli river, Nameri lies remote, inaccessible and incredibly wild. It is flanked on the west by the Balipara Reserve Forest. The forests of Nameri National Park comprise dense semi-evergreen forests, riverine tracts and moist deciduous forests. While being in the forests, you may find Wreathed Hornbills flying overhead. One can also see Tigers roaming these forests & some Wild dogs or dhole as well. Stay Overnight at Nameri.

itinerary.title: Nameri National Park
itinerary.description: Early morning, we will go for the nature walk and the bird watching trip into the dense jungle of Nameri National Park. As many as 233 species of birds have been recorded at Nameri, making it a great place for birdwatchers. The highly endangered White-winged Wood Duck has been spotted in Nameri's riverine forests. The avian diversity includes species such as the Ibisbill, Ruddy Kingfisher, Blue-eared Kingfisher and the very rare Green Cochoa. Other water birds such as the Great Stone Plover, Mallard Pochard and Brahminy ducks are often sighted. King Vultures, Fishing Eagles, Black-necked Storks and other resident and migratory birds are also seen here. Stay Overnight Stay at Nameri.

itinerary.title: Nameri National Park – Guwahati Airport
itinerary.description: After having breakfast at the hotel, you will drive back to Guwahati & will be dropped at the airport for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrival at Guwahati
itinerary.description: Welcome on arrival at Guwahati, meet with our representative and transfer to hotel. On arrival, check into the hotel room. Dinner & Overnight stay in Guwahati.

itinerary.title: Guwahati - Kaziranga National Park [220 Kms/4 hrs]
itinerary.description: In the morning after breakfast visit to Kamakhya Temple, a Major Hindu Pilgrimage site  and later drive to famous Kaziranga National park. On arrival, check into a Jungle resort. After lunch visit nearby Tea Estate .Dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Kaziranga
itinerary.description: On this day start your tour early in the morning (5:30 am) explore central Range of Kaziranga National Park on Elephant Safari. While returning explore the eastern range on a jeep safari. Return to Jungle resort for lunch and then proceed to Western Range of Kaziranga National Park on jeep. Return back to resort in the evening for dinner & Overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Kaziranga - Shillong [280 Kms/6 hrs]
itinerary.description: After an early breakfast transfer to Shillong. Lunch en-route at Borapani Umiam Lake and continue drive. On arrival, check into the hotel. Evening at your own leisure. Enjoy dinner & Overnight at hotel.

itinerary.title: Shillong
itinerary.description: After enjoying breakfast in the hotel drive to Cherrapunjee, the wettest place in the world. The beautiful waterfall Noh-ka-likai is at Cherrapunjee you can also explore some of the caves in and around at Cherrapunjee. Return back to hotel, en-route visit Elephanta falls and Shillong peak. Enjoy your dinner & Overnight at hotel.

itinerary.title: Shillong – Guwahati [100 Kms]
itinerary.description: After a leisure breakfast in the hotel proceed for full day sightseeing of Shillong, including Ward Lake and Hydari Park. Enjoy the beauty of Shillong, it is also called Scotland of the East. In the afternoon drive back to Guwahati. Enjoy cruise on Brahmaputra River in the evening. Dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Guwahati Departure
itinerary.description: After breakfast check out from the hotel. On time, transfer to the Airport/ Railway station for your onward destination.

itinerary.title: Chennai Arrival – Srikalahasti (113 km.)
itinerary.description: The tour begins as you arrive at the international airport of Chennai, the capital of Tamil Nadu. Meet our representative who will escort you directly to the town of Srikalahasti in Andhra Pradesh. The town is home to Srikalahasti (Srikalahastheeswara) Temple which houses Vayu Lingam, the manifestation of air element. Legends state that the Lingam here was worshipped by a spider (sri, who would weave a web to cover the lingam against sunlight, dust and leaves), a snake (kala, who would adorn the lingam with his gem) and an elephant (hasti, who would wash the lingam with water).   It happened one day that the web and the gem were swept away by the water poured by hasti that led to war among the three who ultimately died and were blessed with moksha by Shiva. An interesting highlight of the temple is that the lamp inside constantly flickers though there is no route for the air to enter. Check in at the booked hotel on arrival and visit the temple. Overnight stay at Srikalahasti.

itinerary.title: Srikalahasti – Kanchipuram (127 km.)
itinerary.description: Drive to Kanchipuram in Tamil Nadu which accommodates the second of the Panch Bhoota at Ekambareswarar Temple. Here, Shiva Lingam manifests as the earth element and is called Prithvi Lingam. It was here that Goddess Parvati (Shiva’s consort) performed tough penance under a mango tree to unite with Lord Shiva. To test her dedication, Shiva sent fire and River Ganga to disturb her penance but could not succeed. Finally, Parvati made a lingam out of sand and pleased Shiva. The iconic mango (amb) tree still stands inside the premises and is known to bear four different varieties of the fruit.   Check in at the booked hotel on arrival and pay visit to the temple later. If time permits, you may also visit Kanchi Kamakshi Temple and many other Hindu temples. Overnight stay at Kanchipuram.

itinerary.title: Kanchipuram – Thiruvannamalai (137 km.)
itinerary.description: Leave for Thiruvannamalai where Arunachaleswara (Annamalaiyar) Temple located at the base of the holy Arunachala (Annamalai) Hill is home to Agni (or Jyothi) Lingam, which represents the element fire. The legend associated with the temple states that once Lord Brahma and Lord Vishnu fought for superiority. Lord Shiva appeared in the form of a fire pillar and asked them to find its two ends. While Vishnu couldn’t find the end and accepted the defeat, Brahma lied of finding one and was cursed by Shiva that he would never have a temple on earth. Another legend states that once Goddess Parvati playfully covered Shiva’s eyes with hand and the whole universe was deprived of light. After tough penance by Parvati and other deities, Shiva appeared as a fire pillar over the sacred hill and the light resumed.   On arrival, transfer to the hotel and take rest. Later, worship at the temple and admire the stunning architecture. It is also considered holy to perform circumambulation around the sacred hill. Overnight stay at Thiruvannamalai.

itinerary.title: Thiruvannamalai – Trichy (185 km.)
itinerary.description: Join us to visit the fourth Sthalam which represents the element water and is located at Jambukeshwarar (Jalagandeeswarar) Temple in the form of Appu (Jambu) Lingam in Thiruvanaikaval, a suburb of the city of Trichy (Tiruchirappalli). As per the legends, once Lord Shiva ordered Parvati to descend to Earth and perform penance when the latter mocked his penance. Parvati took the form of Akilandeswari and visited a forest full of jambu trees in the region. Here, she formed a lingam using water of the holy River Kaveri and started her penance. Shiva, pleased by her dedication, appeared before Parvati and blessed her with updeshas (knowledge).   Check in at the booked hotel, relax and later visit the temple. You may also visit Ranganathaswamy Temple and other religious sites depending on the avaiklability of time. Overnight stay at Trichy.

itinerary.title: Trichy – Chidambaram (168 km.)
itinerary.description: Begin the trip to the last destination of the tour. The holy town of Chidambaram is home to Akasha (Aagaya) Lingam which manifests the element sky (or space) and is located at Thillai Nataraja Temple. As the legend goes, Shiva was once walking through the forest of thillai trees in the region in the disguise of a mendicant when his unsurpassable charm and beauty attracted the wives of many sages living in the forest. Enraged at it, the sages sent snakes, tiger, elephant and a powerful demon to attack Shiva, but were defeated in all the attempts. He also performed Tandava, the dance of eternal bliss, taking the form of Nataraja (God of dancers) over the demon’s back. At last, Shiva revealed his true form.  On arrival, check in at the hotel and relax. Later, visit the temple and you may also visit other temples only if time permits. Overnight stay at Chidambaram.

itinerary.title: Chidambaram – Chennai (233 km.)
itinerary.description: Drive back to Chennai and transfer to the airport or railway station to begin the journey back to home.

itinerary.title: Arrive Khajuraho / Satna & transfer to Panna
itinerary.description: You will be met on your arrival at Khajuraho airport / Satna railway station and will be transferred to the hotel in Panna. Situated in the central Indian State of Madhya Pradesh, Panna National Park is home to some of the best wildlife species in India and is one of the famous Tiger Reserves in the country. The park is known worldwide for its wild cats including tigers as well as deer and antelope. The river Ken passes through the park, adding to its scenic beauty. Upon arrival at Panna, we check into the resort and enjoy rest of the day at leisure exploring the local area. Stay overnight at Panna.

itinerary.title: Panna National Park
itinerary.description: Today we will have 02 safaris, morning & afternoon game drives in the park. We will wake-up early in the morning and get ready for our first game drive in the park. Located in Panna and Chhatarpur districts in M.P, Panna National Park is also referred to as a Tiger Reserve since it comes under the aegis of Project Tiger in India. Among the animals found here are the tigers, sloth bear, march crocodile, cinkara, sambhar and chital. The park is home to more than 200 species of birds including the Bar-headed Goose, the Honey Buzzard, the King Vulture and the Blossom-headed Parakeet. Bird life in this park is extremely rich and varied. Later, we return to the resort for breakfast and lunch. In the afternoon, we leave for our second safari for wildlife exploration in Panna National Park. Stay overnight at Panna.

itinerary.title: Transfer to Khajuraho / Satna
itinerary.description: In the morning, we will have our last safari / game drive in Panna National Park. After the safari, return back to the resort & after having breakfast, leave for Khajuraho / Satna & transfer to the airport / railway station to board the flight / train for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Nagpur & transfer to Pench National Park
itinerary.description: You will be met on your arrival at Nagpur airport / railway station and will be transferred to the hotel in Pench National Park. Spread over an area of 758 square kms, Pench National Park is a beautiful area of forest and lightly wooded grassland with many streams and Pench river. Home to a variety of animals, Pench National Park cuddles several wild fauna species and houses many animals like tiger, panther, and many species of deer, wild boar, bison, wild dogs, a variety of monkeys and other animals besides some 250 species of birds. Upon arrival at Pench, we check into the resort and enjoy rest of the day at leisure exploring the local area. Stay overnight at Pench National Park.

itinerary.title: Pench National Park
itinerary.description: Today we will have 02 safaris, morning & afternoon game drives in the park. Bestowed with rich fauna and flora, the Pench National Park has always been among the top destinations for wildlife tours. Early morning, we leave for our first safari in the park and enjoy the sight of different wild animals including the tiger, deer, elephant, gaur, and also treat our eyes by viewing the wonderful vistas formed by both domestic and migratory birds. Be it a mammal, reptile, amphibian or bird, every fauna species that defines wildlife at its best can be seen within this park. Though there are several wild beings residing in the park, it is the tiger that stands as the main attraction. After the safari, we return to the resort for breakfast and lunch. In the afternoon, we leave for our second safari for wildlife exploration in Pench National Park. In addition to this, the Pench National Park also has numerous other natural wonders like Nagdeo Pahadi, Totaladoh Dam, Ambakhori Waterfall (Seasonal), Out Fall (Gawali Ghat) and Meghdoot Jalashaya (Pond). Stay overnight at Pench.

itinerary.title: Transfer to Nagpur
itinerary.description: In the morning, we will have our last safari / game drive in Pench National Park. After the safari, return back to the resort & after having breakfast, leave for Nagpur & transfer to the airport / railway station to board the flight / train for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Kochi & transfer to Periyar
itinerary.description: You will be met on your arrival at Cochin airport /railway station and transferred to the resort in Periyar (175 kms/04 hrs). The Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary is one of the best maintained wildlife sanctuaries in Southern India, giving an opportunity of spotting elephants, leopards, tigers and bisons, amongst various other species of flora and fauna. The park also has a beautiful lake at the heart of the sanctuary which is formed with the building of a dam in 1895. Upon arrival in Periyar, check into the resort and enjoy rest of the time at leisure in the resort. Stay Overnight at Periyar.

itinerary.title: Periyar National Park
itinerary.description: Known as protected wildlife reserve, the Periyar National Park is situated right at the heart of Pandalam Hills and Cardamom Hills of Southern Western Ghats. Early morning, we will go for a jungle walk in Periyar National Park with an opportunity to witness the inhabits of wild animals. After the jungle walk, we come back to the hotel & have breakfast. In the afternoon, we leave for the safari & enjoy a boat cruise on the Periyar Lake. Unlike almost every other National Park in India, Safaris in the Periyar Tiger reserve are mainly conducted by boats in the Lake Periyar. This is an incredible way to experience the Periyar Wildlife sanctuary. The artificial Periyar lake, which encircles the wildlife areas, adds beauty to the sanctuary. In addition to Elephants, the other animals seen in the Periyar sanctuary are Gaur, Wild Pigs, Sambar, Barking Deer, Mouse Deer, Dole or Indian Wild Dog. In the evening, we go for a walking tour to the spice garden that are scattered all over Periyar. Stay overnight at Periyar.

itinerary.title: Periyar National Park – Kochi
itinerary.description: After having breakfast at the hotel, you will drive back to Kochi & will be dropped at the airport /railway station for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Delhi
itinerary.description: Meet our representative on arrival at Delhi. With the assistance offered, reach the hotel and spend a comfortable night.

itinerary.title: Delhi Sightseeing
itinerary.description: The actual tour begins as you get ready after the breakfast and join us for the full day sightseeing of the national capital. Visit topmost attractions of Old and New Delhi, including Lotus Temple and the temples of Laxmi Narayan and Akshardham, Red Fort, Qutub Minar, Humayun’s Tomb and Jama Masjid among others. Get back to the hotel late evening.

itinerary.title: Delhi - Agra (205 km. /4.5 hr.)
itinerary.description: Get ready for the early morning drive to Agra, enjoying breakfast on the route. Before checking in at the hotel, visit Sikandra Fort. Check in and relax, before we take you to the unforgettable rendezvous with the very majestic Taj Mahal. Pay visit to the colourful handicraft bazaars in the evening and enjoy at leisure. Take dinner at the hotel.

itinerary.title: Agra - Fatehpur Sikri – Jaipur
itinerary.description: Finish breakfast and begin the drive to Jaipur. Enjoy an amazing halt at the historical city of Fatehpur Sikri, boasting of fascinating red sandstone and marble structures like palaces, royal residences, halls, mosques and more. After this spellbinding expedition, proceed to Jaipur and check in at the hotel on arrival. Relax till the dinner is served.

itinerary.title: Jaipur Sightseeing
itinerary.description: After breakfast, you will be first visiting the Amber Fort perched on a hilltop. Enjoy the thrilling elephant ride to the fort and explore the beautiful royal palaces, temples and flower gardens. After this outstanding experience, get ready for another at Chokhi Dhani in the evening. Relish the delicious Rajasthan cuisines at this ethnic restaurant while enjoying the entertaining dance and music performances. Return to the hotel.

itinerary.title: Jaipur – Pushkar
itinerary.description: Today, you will be escorted to Pushkar after breakfast. Visit the world-renowned Dargah of Sufi Saint Salim Chisti at Ajmer on the way and feel the spell of the overwhelming peace. Proceed to Pushkar and check in at the hotel on arrival. After some rest, visit the holy Pushkar Lake and the temple of Lord Brahma. Get back to the hotel after a visit to the Rose Farm and have dinner.

itinerary.title: Pushkar Sightseeing
itinerary.description: Enjoy breakfast and spend the whole day at leisure, enjoying various competitions, activities and performances at the fair. It will be the night of full moon (called Poornima) that adds splendour to the desert canvas.

itinerary.title: Pushkar – Delhi
itinerary.description: Check out after breakfast and drive back to Delhi. Transfer directly to the airport or the railway station on arrival to board flight or train for the final journey to home. The tour ends here.

itinerary.title: Arrival at Delhi
itinerary.description:
Our representative shall welcome you on arrival at Delhi airport and shall assist you in transfer to the hotel booked by you. 

Overnight stay shall be at the hotel.

itinerary.title: Delhi to Ranthambore
itinerary.description:
After an early morning breakfast, transfer to railway station to catch train for Sawai Madhopur,Rajasthan.

 On arrival at Sawai Madhopur railway station, our representative shall meet you and assist you in transfer to the hotel or resort booked for you. 

Evening shall be at leisure & overnight stay shall be at hotel or resort.

itinerary.title: Ranthambore National Park
itinerary.description: Enjoy the morning and evening park safari at Ranthambore National Park.  Enjoy sumptuous meals at hotel or resort.Overnight stay at hotel or resort.

itinerary.title: Ranthambore to Delhi
itinerary.description:
In the morning after another opportunity of safari in the park transfer to Sawai Madhopur Railway station on time to catch train for Delhi. 

 On arrival at Delhi Railway Station meet our representative who would assist you in transfer to the hotel/ Airport to catch flight for onward destination.

    Tour ends with memories to last forever...

itinerary.title: Arrive Sawai Madhopur & transfer to Ranthambore National Park
itinerary.description: You will be met on your arrival at Sawai Madhopur railway station and will be transferred to the resort in Ranthambore National Park. Situated in Sawai Madhopur, Ranthambore National Park is one of the biggest national parks in Rajasthan which covers an area of 392 km², and is one of India's Project Tiger reserves. Ranthambore National Park is known for large tiger population and is one of the best places in India to see these majestic predators in the forest. Upon arrival, check into the resort & relax in a serene ambiance. In the evening, we may wish to visit the famous Ranthambore Fort or to explore the local area. Stay overnight at Ranthambore.

itinerary.title: Ranthambore National Park
itinerary.description: Today we will have 02 safaris, morning & afternoon game drives in the park. An early morning drive to the dense jungle of Ranthambore with naturalist for tiger tracking and explore wildlife. Explore the mystery of the tiger reserve with naturalist in an open top roof canter. While cruising through the jungle there is strong probability to track the legend tiger roaming around. After the safari, we return to the resort for breakfast and lunch. There is time to relax and enjoy the facilities of your hotel, and revive your mind and body by taking dip in the pool for a break from the heat of the day. In the afternoon, we leave for our second safari for wildlife exploration in Ranthambore National Park. Other major wild animals include leopard, nilgai, dhole, wild boar, sambar, hyena, sloth bear and chital. Stay overnight at Ranthambore.

itinerary.title: Transfer to Sawai Madhopur
itinerary.description: In the morning, we will have our last safari / game drive in Ranthambore National Park. After the safari, return back to the resort & after having breakfast, leave for Sawai Madhopur & transfer to the railway station to board the flight / train for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Arrive Jaipur & transfer to the hotel in Sariska
itinerary.description: You will be met on your arrival at Jaipur airport / railway station and will be transferred to the hotel in Sariska. Nestled in the Aravali Hills, Sariska Tiger Reserve covers 800 sq km area divided into the grasslands, dry deciduous forests, sheer cliffs and rocky landscape. It is home to India's largest population of peafowl, and harbours quail, sand grouse, golden- backed woodpeckers and crested serpent eagles, among other species. Upon arrival, check into the hotel & relax for rest of the day. Stay overnight at Sariska.

itinerary.title: Sariska National Park
itinerary.description: Today we will have 02 safaris in the park. Famous for majestic tigers, Sariska National Park is abode of large number of wildlife and diverse variety of birds. One can also sight a tiger by taking up a jeep safari or elephant safari. Morning Jungle drive in the wildlife sanctuary to see diverse species of flora and fauna. Other predators of the forest include the panther, jungle cat, jackal, hyena, and fox. Also found are sambhar, chital, wild boar, hare, nilgai, civet, four-horned antelope, gaur (Indian bison) and porcupine. In the afternoon, we again leave for a Jungle safari to view the numerous carnivores including Leopard, Wild Dog, Jungle Cat, and Tiger. Stay overnight at Sariska.

itinerary.title: Transfer to Jaipur
itinerary.description: After having breakfast at the hotel, drive by surface to Jaipur and transfer to the airport / railway station to board the flight / train for your onward journey.

itinerary.title: Reach Srinagar Airport
itinerary.description:
Early morning at about 6 am reach Srinagar Airport from where our representative shall escort you to the helicopter.

 Board the helicopter to Panjtarni from where you can take pony/palki at your own cost.From Panjtarni the distance of the holy Amarnath caves is about 6 Kms.Reach the holy Amarnath caves and after having the Darshan at the Ice Lingam, return back to Pantarni.

From Panjtarni, our helicopter shall fly you back to Srinagar Airport from where you can take your scheduled flight to your onward city.

  Please note that although registration is not compulsory for the Yatris visiting Amarnath by helicopter, a medical certificate from one of the Doctors / Medicals Institutions authorized to issue compulsory health certificate shall be a must.Further, please note that children below the age of 13 and adults above the age of 75 are not allowed to undertake Amarnath Yatra. For more details you may refer the website of Shri Amarnath Shrine Board on the link.

itinerary.title: Arrive Kolkata & transfer to Sundarbans National Park
itinerary.description: Arrive at Kolkata airport / railway station and drive to Godhkhali, a journey of about 3 hrs. We transfer you from Godhkhali jetty by   motor boat to the resort/hotel located in the Sundarbans Islands. Later, we set off on motor boat and view wildlife from the   Sajnekhali watchtower and visit the Sajnekhali Museum and Mangrove Interpretation Centre. Most of the tigers are sighted from this   tower. Some other wildlife like axis deers, wild boars and crocodiles may also be seen from this watch tower. Stay Overnight at   hotel/resort.

itinerary.title: Sundarbans National Park
itinerary.description: The second day involves cruising through the deep creeks and covering the watchtowers of Burir Dabri, Kumirmari as well as   Morich Jhapi. Burirdabri is famous for its watchtower, a mudwalk and mangrove cage trail leading to a viewpoint known as   Raimongal View Point. The Kumirmari watchtower offers an exciting view of the jungles. Overlooking an untamed forests cape, enjoy   the sights and sounds of nature resplendent in her pristine virginal beauty. You can also take a stroll through the Kumirmari bazaar   if you want to have a first-hand view of a typical rustic Indian market place. Stay Overnight at hotel/resort.

itinerary.title: Sundarbans National Park
itinerary.description: Enjoy full day forest excursion through the creeks of riverine way of the dense forest & explore the largest mangrove forest of the   world - Sundarbans. The Netidhopani watchtower offers one a view of the ruins of a 400 year old Shiva Temple. It is also believed   that there is a road on the extreme right of the forest which was built by King Pratapaditya to guard the coastal area. It is now   covered with earth. There is also a sweet water pond. This watch tower has a capacity to host 20 persons at a time.

itinerary.title: Sundarbans National Park – Kolkata
itinerary.description: If time allows, we set off for a visit to a village and learn about the experiences of the villagers in the forest. Meet the fishermen, the   Honey Collectors as well as the woodcutters and share their experiences of the Jungle. After lunch at the resort/boat, we depart for   Godhkhali to be transferred to Kolkata. Arrive at Kolkata & transfer to the railway station/airport.

itinerary.title: Arrive Nagpur & transfer to Tadoba National Park
itinerary.description: You will be met on your arrival at Nagpur airport / railway station and will be transferred to the hotel in Tadoba National Park. Located in Chandrapur district of Maharashtra state, Tadoba National Park also known as Tadoba Andhari Tiger Reserve, is one of the finest & largest National Parks in India. This beautiful wild paradise is situated 45 km close to Chandrapur District of Central Indian State Maharashtra, and is about 150 km from Nagpur city which is the nearest airport for visiting Tadoba national park. Famous for its natural heritage, the Reserve contains some of the best of forest tracks and endowed with rich biodiversity. Forest vegetation is mainly mixed type with good presence of teak and bamboo forest. It is dry deciduous forest having undulating and rolling terrain, with small lakes which ensures water availability in summers. Upon arrival, we check into the resort and relax for rest of the day. Stay overnight at Tadoba.

itinerary.title: Tadoba National Park
itinerary.description: Today we will have 02 safaris, morning & afternoon game drives in the park. Often referred to as The Jewel of Vidharba, the Tadoba National Park lies in the heart of a reserved forest, it is an infinite treasure trove of innumerable species of trees and plants. Early morning, we leave for our first safari in the park and enjoy the sight of wildlife that includes tigers, panthers, sloth bears, hyenas, jackals, wild dogs, bison, barking deer, nil gai, sambar, and cheatal. Similarly, presence of lakes ensures good birding with some commonly seen birds like Indian Roller, Black-headed Oriole, Black Drongo, Red-vented Bulbul, Green Bee-eater, Golden Oriole, Rose-ringed Parakeet, Cormorants, Eagrets, Herons, Sunbirds etc. After the safari, we return to the resort for breakfast and lunch. In the afternoon, we leave for our second safari for wildlife exploration in Tadoba National Park. Out of 41 mammals species, some commonly seen mammals are Tigers, Sloth Bear, Jackal, Sambar, Spotted Dear, Wild Boar, Four-horned Antelope, Indian Gaur, Wild dog etc. Stay overnight at Tadoba.

itinerary.title: Transfer to Nagpur
itinerary.description: In the morning, we will have our last safari / game drive in Tadoba National Park. After the safari, return back to the resort & after having breakfast, leave for Nagpur & transfer to the airport / railway station to board the flight / train for your onward journey.

title: The Best Of Kashmir Holiday Package

itinerary.title: Srinagar Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrive at Srinagar Airport where our tour representative will welcome you. Transfer to the houseboat at Dal Lake and rest for some time. Spend time exploring the enthralling beauty dominating the place before the dinner is served.

itinerary.title: Srinagar Sightseeing
itinerary.description: Start this exciting day with breakfast before we guide you through the beautiful Mughal Gardens of the city. You will be spellbound by the well-planned and lush green Nishat and Shalimar Gardens which enjoy the backdrops of the snow-capped peaks. Return to the houseboat for lunch and get ready for the extraordinary Shikara boat ride on the Dal Lake. Feel blessed by nature’s eternal magnificence and enjoy the views of many popular monuments at the shores. End the day with breathtaking sunset views before getting back to the houseboat.

itinerary.title: Srinagar – Gulmarg – Srinagar (52 km. each side)
itinerary.description: Today, the breakfast will be followed by the excursion to Gulmarg, the meadow of flowers, known for its world-class skiing slopes and golf course. Have some remarkable views of the snow-capped mountains and spectacular landscapes. The highlight of the trip would be the thrilling gondola cable car ride which will take you to great heights for splendid views of the region rightly termed as the paradise. Get back to the houseboat by evening.

itinerary.title: Srinagar – Pahalgam – Srinagar (97 km. each side)
itinerary.description: Get up early, have breakfast and follow the route to Pahalgam. Visit the eye-pleasing saffron fields and the must-visit Sulphur Springs at Anantnag on the way. You will also visit the captivating ruins of Vishnu Temple at Avantipur before reaching Pahalgam. Explore the fascinating valleys, sprawling meadows and locations popular among the Indian filmmakers. Drive back to Srinagar and reach the houseboat in the evening.

itinerary.title: Srinagar – Sonmarg – Srinagar (82 km. each side)
itinerary.description: Have breakfast and get into the car for today’s excursion which includes the unforgettable visit to Sonmarg, the meadows of gold. Get awe-struck by the beauty of this alpine valley located on the bank of Sindh River. You may opt to ride a pony and visit Thajiwas Glacier which remains covered with snow forever. After enjoying this inimitable experience, drive back to the houseboat at Srinagar.

itinerary.title: Srinagar Departure
itinerary.description: After breakfast, you will be dropped to the airport where you will board the flight to your destination and end the tour.

itinerary.title: Arrive Kochi
itinerary.description:
Welcome on arrival at Cochin Airport/ Railway Station, meet our tour representative and transfer to the hotel.
 Cochin or Kochi is located on the southwest coast of Indian Peninsula. It is also known as "Queen of the Arabian Sea" and "The Gateway to Kerala".
 In the afternoon proceed for a half day tour of Cochin including Jewish Synagogue Dutch Palace, St. Francis Church, Chinese fishing nets and Bolgatty Palace.
 Return back to the hotel in the evening for dinner & Overnight stay at the hotel.

itinerary.title: Kochi - Trichur - Kochi [Day of Thrissur Elephant Festival]
itinerary.description:
After a healthy breakfast drive to Trichur to witness the famous Temple festival of India.
 Thrissur Elephant festival is celebrated annually in the month of April - May as per the Malayalam Calendar.
 This festival is referred to as Thrissur Pooram in Thrissur and is considered as the festival of all festivals in Kerala since there are many festivals held in Kerala where elephants are involved in ceremonies and processions but the Thrissur Pooram is held with the largest gathering of decorated elephants and a sea of humanity that descends on Thrissur.
 Return back to Hotel in the evening for dinner & Overnight stay at the hotel in Cochin

itinerary.title: Kochi - Alleppey (Houseboat)
itinerary.description:
In the morning after breakfast, transfer to Houseboat in Alleppey.
 Reach and settle down at houseboat.
 Rest of the day free at leisure. Sit back and relax while the boat cruises through the narrow serene backwater canals. Have Lunch, Evening Tea/Coffee with snacks and enjoy your dinner & Overnight stay on the houseboat.

itinerary.title: Alleppey - Kochi
itinerary.description:
Enjoy leisure on House Boat experience the pollution free air and after enjoying breakfast check out from the houseboat.
 Transfer on time to the Airport/Railway station at Cochin.

itinerary.title: Mumbai Arrival
itinerary.description: The tour begins with your arrival at the Mumbai Airport, from where you will be transferred to the hotel by our representative. After checking in, take some rest and spend the rest of the day at leisure, exploring the most happening metropolitan city of India.

itinerary.title: Mumbai Sightseeing
itinerary.description: Have breakfast at the hotel and get ready for the full day sightseeing tour. Explore the colonial heritage at the Gateway of India and the Prince of Wales Museum and visit the very popular Siddhivinayak Temple. Later visit the lively beaches of Juhu and Chaupati. Take leisurely stroll along the sea at the Marine Drive and enjoy the regressing sea breeze. Return to the hotel.

itinerary.title: Mumbai Sightseeing
itinerary.description: After breakfast, you will be transferred to the jetty to board the ferry to Elephanta Caves. Located on a pristine island, the caves dating back to the 6th century are rich in captivating sculptures. The boat ride itself is a thrilling experience. Get back to the city and spend evening at leisure before returning to the hotel.

itinerary.title: Mumbai-Bangalore
itinerary.description: After check out, you will be transferred to the airport to board the scheduled flight to Bangalore, the city known for its high-tech industry, gardens and monuments. The tour representative will receive you at the airport and transfer you to the hotel. Check-in and relax. You will be free to spend the remaining day at leisure.

itinerary.title: Bangalore Sightseeing
itinerary.description: Finish your breakfast and proceed for a soulful journey through the city’s heritage trail. You will be visiting the attractions like Tipu Sultan’s Summer Palace, Lalbagh, Cubbon Park, Bull Temple, Gani Gangadhareshwara Temple and VIdhana Soudha. Get back to the hotel at the tour end.

itinerary.title: Bangalore-Puttaparthi (155 km.)
itinerary.description: Have breakfast and check out from the hotel. Join drive to the spiritual town of Puttaparthi, home to the internationally acclaimed Prasanthi Nilayam Ashram of Sri Sathya Sai Baba. Visit the ashram after check-in at the hotel and let your mind and soul soak in the overpowering peace while feeling connected to the supreme. Sathya Sai Space Theatre and Chaianya Jyoti Ashram are other attractions you may like to visit before returning to the hotel.

itinerary.title: Puttaparthi-Tirupati (225 km.)
itinerary.description: Have breakfast early morning and take the route to Tirupati, a famous pilgrimage destination in South India. Check in at the hotel on arrival and take some rest. Later, visit the acclaimed temple of Venkateswara Swamy and seek blessings from the lord. Return to the hotel at night.

itinerary.title: Tirupati-Chennai (157 km.)
itinerary.description: Enjoy breakfast before we help you to check out and move to the city of Chennai. Check in at the hotel and take rest. For the remaining day, you can plan to explore the metropolitan city at your own will.

itinerary.title: Chennai Sightseeing
itinerary.description: Have breakfast before we take you for the full day city tour, exploring the amazing hotspots like Fort St. George, San Thomas Basilica and Kapaleeswarar Temple. Later, spend some relaxing moments at the beautiful Marina Beach before we drop you back to the hotel.

itinerary.title: Chennai-Kanchipuram-Mahabalipuram
itinerary.description: After breakfast, join the excursion to the temple town of Kachipuram. Explore the majestic temples of Kamakshi Amman and Ekambareswarar. Later, drive to the town of Mahabalipuram, home to the enthralling UNESCO World Heritage Sites. You will be left speechless while exploring the rock-cut temples and monuments located near the seashore. Panch Rathas (Five Chariots), Shore Temple and many other highlights keep you engaged for long, before you get back to the hotel at Chennai.

itinerary.title: Chennai Departure
itinerary.description: After you have breakfast and check out from the hotel, we will transfer you to the international airport. Board the flight to end the memorable tour.

itinerary.title: Mumbai – Trimbakeshwar (178 km.) – Aurangabad (211 km.)
itinerary.description:
Panch Jyotirlinga tour from Mumbai(also famously called by some as Mumbai Jyotirlinga tour) begins with your arrival in Mumbai, the state capital.

 As pre-decided, our Jyotirlinga tour representative meets you at the airport or railway station or any other location. We immediately begin the journey by road to the town of Trimbak, the first destination of the Jyotirlinga list.

 On arrival, visit Trimbakeshwar Jyotirlinga temple which houses a Shiva Lingam with 3 small lingams embodying the faces of Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva(Mahesh).

 As per the legend, Sage Gautama, in order to get rid of the sin of accidentally killing a cow performed penance to Lord Shiva who appeared at the nearby Brahmagiri Hill and commanded River Ganga to flow as River Godavari from here. When the river, disappointed at leaving the lord, appeared and disappeared at different places, Gautama managed to stop it at Kushavarta, a water tank still present in the temple premises. The sage bathed in the tank to wipe off his sin and prayed to Shiva to stay here forever and he complied by appearing in the form of the Lingam.

   The temple opens from 05:30 a.m. to 09:00 p.m. and is famous for the most revered place in India to perform pujas to get rid of horoscope doshas (curses) like Pitru Dosha and Kalsarpa Dosha. It is worth mentioning that a highly precious crown (with emeralds, diamonds and other jewels) is displayed over the Lingam every Monday. Visitors are only allowed to have darshan of the lingam from a distance and only male visitors who book special poojas are allowed to touch it after wearing the instructed dress.

   Later, leave Trimbak to start the road journey to Aurangabad. On arrival, check in at the booked hotel and stay overnight.

itinerary.title: Aurangabad – Aundha Nagnath (199 km.) – Parli (119 km.) – Aurangabad (214 km.)
itinerary.description:
Start the day early and join us on the road trip to the second among the Panch Jyotirlinga tour from Mumbai, it is Aundha Nagnath Jyotirlinga which is located in the town by the same name.

 According to the legend, Lord Shiva decided to stay here in the form of a lingam to protect the people from a demon named Daruka. Another popular story associated with the temple is that when the eminent Saint Namdev was denied to sing bhajans (songs praising God) at the front of the temple by the priests, he went to the back of the temple and started singing there. However, the Almighty who was impressed by his singing rotated the temple by 180 degrees. This is the reason that the statue of Nandi, Shiva's Bull, which usually faces the Lingam is found at the back of this temple.

   The temple opens from 04:00 a.m. to 09:00 p.m.

 Pray at the temple and admire its stunning carvings.

 Later, start the road trip to Parli Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga which is located in the town of Parli on the mountain named Meru and near three ancient rivers named Venu, Brahma and Saraswati.

 The legend states that when Amrit (holy nectar) and Dhanavantri (the physician of Gods) emerged out of Sagar Manthan (churning of the ocean) performed by Gods and demons, Lord Vishnu hid them in the Shiva Lingam at Parli to save them from demons. When demons tried to touch the lingam, the flames erupted but Amrit flew out of it when the Gods and devotees touched the lingam. Even today, the lingam is believed to empower devotees who touch it with the holy nectar and healing powers. This is the reason that it is known as Vaidyanath (or Lord of Physicians). The entire region is full of herbs with medicinal powers.

   Pray at the temple which opens from 05:00 a.m. to 09:00 p.m. (closes between 03:30-06:00 p.m.). Then, start the return trip to Aurangabad for night stay.

itinerary.title: Aurangabad – Grishneshwar (31 km.) - Shani Shingnapur (102 km.) – Shirdi (73 km.)
itinerary.description:
In the early morning, we start the road trip to Grishneshwar Jyotirlinga Temple(the fourth among the Panch Jyotirlinga tour from Mumbai) which is located in a small village called Verul.

 On arrival, visit the temple which opens from 05:30 a.m. to 09:00 p.m.

 The mythological story associated with the temple states that an ardent Shiva devote named Ghushma lived in the area along with her husband and his second wife named Sudeha. Ghushma used to worship Lord Shiva by making 101 Lingams and immersing them in a lake every single day. Once, Sudeha kills Ghushma's son out of jealousy and throws his body in the same lake. When Ghushma came to know about his son's absence, she was not moved even a bit and believed firmly that Shiva will protect her son. Her belief came true as his son returned soon in perfectly fine condition. Then, Ghushma requests Lord Shiva to forgive Sudeha and stay there forever. Shiva granted her wishes and established himself as the lingam.

   Pray at the temple which is among few Shiva Jyotirlinga temples where devotees can actually touch the lingam.

 Later, start the journey to the village of Shani Shingnapur which is home to the popular temple dedicated to God Shani (associated with the planet Saturn). The residents of the village don't put doors or locks to their properties as they firmly believe that no theft or robbery is possible due to the presence of Shani Dev in the village.

 On arrival, visit the shrine which houses the swayambhu (self-manifested) stone image of God Shani. The Devasthan, where the image is installed in open, stays open for darshan 24 hours a day. If time allows, you may stay till sunset to attend the evening mass aarti.

   Continue the road trip and reach the village of Shirdi which hosts one of the world's most visited religious sites called Sai Baba Temple (opens till 11:00 p.m.).

 The temple is built around the Samadhi of Sai Baba, the respected saint and guru who has millions of followers from all religions and faiths. Check in at the booked hotel and pray at the temple and visit other popular places associated with Baba's life. These include Dwarkamai (Baba's dwelling place), Chawdi (where he would sleep every alternative night), Khandoba Temple, Gurusthan (where Baba was first spotted in the state of meditation) and Museum (housing various articles used by him).

 Stay overnight at Shirdi.

itinerary.title: Shirdi – Bhimashankar (185 km.) – Pune (110 km.)
itinerary.description:
On the fourth day of Panch Jyotirlinga tour from Mumbai, you would leave Shirdi and travel by road to .

 Located in the Sahaydri Range Western Ghats amidst lush green surroundings, the temple isolates itself from the chaotic world. It is believed that Lord Shiva established himself as a Jyotirlinga after killing the demon Bhima. Another legend states that Lord Shiva killed the demon Tipurasur here after a long battle. River Bhima which starts its journey near the temple is believed to have been formed by Shiva's sweat after the battle.

   Visit the temple (open from 04:30 a.m. to 09:30 p.m.) and pray to the lingam. You would also be stunned by the magnificent Nagara Architectural style of the temple. Later, start the road trip to Pune and check in at the booked hotel on arrival.

itinerary.title: Pune – Mumbai (150 km.) - Departure
itinerary.description: After breakfast, we start our drive from Pune to Mumbai. On arrival, we help you transfer to the airport or railway station after which the Jyotirlinga in Maharashtra itinerary ends.

itinerary.title: Delhi - Dharamsala
itinerary.description:
Early in the morning, drive to Dharamsala. On arrival, check into the hotel and relax. Dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

  Did you know ?
 A lesser known fact about Dharamshala and McLeodganj is that these destinations have Yoga Institutes and Spas offering short duration courses in Yoga and wellness packages.So, if you wish to improve your health in the lap of Himalayas, book one of these packages.Notable among these institutes are Himalayan Iyengar Yoga Centre in Dharamkot, which is about 6 Kms from Dharamshala.Other famous once are Om Yoga Meditation Centre at Mcleodganj and Siddhartha Yoga Centre.

itinerary.title: Dharamsala
itinerary.description:
After a healthy breakfast, proceed for full day sightseeing of Dharamshala.

 Sightseeing in your trip to Dalhousie from Delhi may include the Tibetan Monastery, St. John's church in the wilderness, Triund Peak, Udechee huts, Dal Lake, Naddi.

 In afternoon visit McLeod Ganj and enjoy the beauty of the nature on the earth. Return back to hotel for dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Dharamsala - Dalhousie
itinerary.description:
After breakfast in the hotel, checkout and drive to Dalhousie.

 On arrival check into the hotel and later visit Panchpula, a beautiful spot  which is barely 3 kms from G.P.O, Subhash Baoli, just about 1 km from G.P.O. which is a charming natural spot with tall pine trees.Also visit Laxmi Narayan Temple, which is dedicated to Lord Vishnu and was built more than 150 years ago.

 Later return back to hotel for dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Day 04: Dalhousie
itinerary.description:
After enjoying breakfast in the hotel, visit Khajjiar & Chamba.Visit Khajji Naga Shrine, Khajjiar Lake, Chaugan in Chamba, Rang Mahal, Rock Garden at Devi Dehra.

 Later drive back to hotel for dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Day 05: Dalhousie - Delhi
itinerary.description:
After an early breakfast drive to Delhi. On arrival, transfer to your convenient place for onward destination.
 
End of tour services.

itinerary.title: Delhi - Amritsar
itinerary.description:
On Arrival at Amritsar airport or railway station, transfers to the hotel.

  On reaching the hotel, check in and savour some delicious Punjabi cuisine. Take rest in the afternoon and in the evening, head out and take a trip to Golden Temple (open 03 a.m. – 10 p.m.).

 Known to Sikhs as Harmandir Sahib (the Temple of God), the legendary Golden Temple is considered to be the holiest Gurdwara for Sikhs all over the world. It is also a symbol of human equality and brotherhood. The water tank reveredly called as Amrit Sarovar which is all around the main Gurudwara was excavated by the fourth Sikh guru, Ram Das, in the year 1577. 

 Anybody, irrespective of their cast, race or creed can seek religious fulfilment and spiritual solace without any hindrance. 

 It also represents the distinct identity, heritage and glory of the Sikhs. To pen-down the ideology, philosophy, the outer and inner beauty, as well as the historical legacy of Sri Harmandir Sahib is a portentous task. Just be certain to wash your hands and feet before entering the holy complex. Covering the head for both males and females is mandatory. 

 The Golden Temple is set amidst a pool of holy water. The focus of attention is the tank – the Amrit Sarovar that surrounds the gleaming central shrine. This is where Amritsar gets its name as well. Ringed by a marble walkway, the tank is said to have curative powers, and pilgrims come from across the world to bathe in its hallowed waters.If you wish, you can take a dip in the holy water but swimming or use of soaps and shampoos is strictly prohibited. 

 On this trip to Golden Temple, you can get breath-taking snapshots of the sunrise and sunset. Just remember that photography is allowed only in the outer premises and not inside the Gurdwara. 

 You will also get a glimpse of the Sikh temple tradition of offering daily Guru-Ka-Langar. It is a free, community meal, a concept that was coined in the 16th century. You will be thrilled to watch the community kitchen feed up to 10,000 people daily. Grab a meal here and learn about this Sikh code of equality and the practice of eating together. 

 Return to the hotel for dinner and spend the night at the hotel in Amritsar grabbing some good night’s sleep.

itinerary.title: Amritsar
itinerary.description:
Start the second day of your customized package of Golden Temple trip with Amristar sightseeing early with a healthy breakfast. 

 Then, set out to visit the Wagha Border. It is the border line running along the Grand Trunk Road between Amritsar in India’s Punjab and Punjab in Lahore, Pakistan. 

 Witness the Beating Retreat ceremony held by the Indian BSF and the Pakistani Rangers.The ceremony commences at 4:15 p.m. in winter and 5:15 p.m. in summer. It lasts for about 45 minutes. You need to arrive at least an hour prior to the beginning of the ceremony as not everyone can get in. No tickets are needed for the event. 

 This ceremony comprises of the closing of the international gates between both countries and lowering the flags. Every evening, just before the sunset there is a thirty minute display of military showmanship. The ceremony is utterly gripping, one which leaves you full of patriotism and pride. 

While returning, drive up to the Shera Wali Gate and then take a rickshaw ride upto the Jallianwala Bagh (open 06.30 a.m. – 07.30 p.m.). It is a public garden which is also home to a memorial that commemorates the massacre of peaceful celebrators by the British armies on 13th April, 1961. Today, the place has been converted into a beautiful park and is superintended by the Jallianwala Bagh National memorial trust. 

 Next, visit the Hall Bazaar. It easily qualifies for being one of the busiest markets in the city of Amritsar. You can purchase electronic items, best quality books, ornaments, handicrafts and ready-made garments. Do not miss the view of its gigantic Mughal style gate studded with a giant clock with the caption Amritsar - Sifti Da Ghar.

 Spend the overnight at the hotel in Amritsar after a delicious meal.

itinerary.title: Amritsar - Delhi
itinerary.description:
Have a lip smacking breakfast on your last day of this trip to Golden Temple.

 Check out from the hotel and meet our tour representative. Transfer to the airport or any other drop point will be arranged from where you begin the journey back to home. Your Golden Temple trip ends here.

itinerary.title: Delhi - Dehradun [Kms 240/5 hrs]
itinerary.description: Pick up from your convenient place and drive to Dehradun. On arrival check-in into the hotel and relax. Rest of the day at your own leisure. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Dehradun Travel
itinerary.description: Second day of Dehradun travel, After enjoying the breakfast in the hotel then proceed for full day local sightseeing & excursion of Dehradun. Explore the local market in the evening during trip to Dehradun and back to the hotel for dinner & overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Dehradun - Delhi
itinerary.description: Last day of trip to Dehradun, After breakfast on time drive to Delhi by surface for onward journey.

itinerary.title: Delhi – Shimla [350 Kms/9 hrs]
itinerary.description:
Early morning, we would pick you up from your convenient place and drive directly to Shimla.

 Alternatively, in case you wish to enjoy a ride on the toy train to Shimla, you may leave Delhi a night before at about 10:30 pm in the evening for Kalka railway station.This way, you would board the train to Shimla early in the morning, while the car will drive further to Shimla to pick you from Shimla railway station upon your arrival at Shimla.This train journey on your trip to Shimla, which crosses about 102 tunnels between Kalka and Shimla will leave an indelible mark on your memory to cherish rest of your life.

  On arrival at Shimla check into the hotel.

 Rest of the day at your own leisure. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Shimla Trip
itinerary.description:
On the second day of your trip to Shimla,after enjoying breakfast proceed for full day sightseeing of Shimla.

 Visit the famous temples, churches, hills, museum, Kufri, Chail, Fagu, shops & malls during Shimla tour. 

 Later, return back to hotel in the evening for dinner & Overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Shimla - Delhi
itinerary.description:
On the last day of your trip to Shimla, after breakfast ,leave for a visit to Naldhera, Mashobra & Tattapani.

  Late drive to Delhi, on arrival transfer to your place for onwards destination.

title: Udaipur And Mount Abu Tour Package

itinerary.title: Arrival at Udaipur
itinerary.description:
Upon arrival at Udaipur airport or railway station, our representative would welcome you and would take you to your hotel.

 After getting refreshed, go for city tour of Udaipur, the city of lakes. Visit City Palace and museum, Jagdish temple, Saheliyon ki Badi, Vintage car museum.

 In the evening, enjoy boat ride at Lake Pichola.

 Overnight stay at Udaipur.

itinerary.title: Full day tour of Kumbhalgarh, Ranakpur and Haldi Ghati
itinerary.description:
After an early breakfast, go for a full day tour of Udaipur region.

 Visit historic fort of Kumbhalgarh Fort, Ranakpur Jain Temples and famous battlefield of Haldi Ghati.

 Return back to Udaipur in the evening and rest of time free for shopping.

 Overnight stay at Udaipur.

itinerary.title: Udaipur - Mount Abu (168 Km / appx. 4 hrs.)
itinerary.description:
After early breakfast, proceed to Mount Abu, the only hill station in Rajasthan.

 Upon arrival at Mount Abu, check in at the hotel. After getting refreshed, go for sightseeing of Mount Abu.

 In this tour, visit beautiful Dilwara temples, Toad rock, Sunset point etc.

 Also, visit Nakki lake and here you can enjoy a boat ride.

 Overnight stay at Mount Abu.

itinerary.title: Departure from Mount Abu
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, transfer to Udaipur airport or Udaipur / Abu Road railway station as per your onward connection.

 End of a memorable tour!!

itinerary.title: Arrive at Udaipur
itinerary.description:
Welcome to the Lake City!

 Your Udaipur city tour package just got better with a lake-view hotel. After resting, gear up for an eventful day of sightseeing around this charming city.

 The first attraction on your Udaipur package tour is City Palace. It stands magnificently on the banks of Lake Pichola. The Mahals, corridors, terraces, rooms, gardens at the City Palace display some finest Rajput elements of art and grandeur.

 The next stop on your Udaipur city tour package is the majestic Saheliyon-ki-Bari which is on the banks of Fateh Sagar Lake. The garden has fountains and marble statues with a picturesque view.

 In the evening, visit the Doodh Talai musical garden to experience a magical sunset. Also, visit the Karni Mata Temple which is accessible through tramway from Dhoodh Talai. If time permits, enjoy a boat ride in the stunning Lake Pichola.

 Head back to the hotel for dinner and night stay.

itinerary.title: Visit Haldighati, Kumbhalgarh Fort and Ranakpur
itinerary.description:
Start your second day of Udaipur package tour with some traditional Rajasthani breakfast.

  Then proceed to the first destination for the day, Haldighati, located 40 km from Udaipur.

 This place is widely known for a battle between Rajputs and Mughals. There is a memorial of Maharana Pratap's famous horse Chetak. You can shop for local artifacts from cottage industry and Mulela art.

 Next, head on to visit the UNESCO Heritage Site, Kumbhalgarh Fort. This fort has enormous gates and massive walls which extend up to 36 km. The glorious complex has various palaces, gardens and more than 360 temples.

Later, visit the Jain temples of Ranakpur which are applauded for its intricate marble carvings and majestic architecture.

 The temple is adorned with 24 pillared huge halls and 80 domes. This wonder has many other temples with superb architecture which you should not miss. Enjoy another peaceful evening of your Udaipur city tour package in this gorgeous temple.

 Return back to Udaipur for dinner and overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Trip to Eklingji and Nathdwara
itinerary.description:
Wake up to a peaceful morning with a lake view and have breakfast.

 Post that, get ready for a trip to the famous Eklingji Temple on your third day of Udaipur city tour package.

 It takes an hour to reach this famous temple devoted to Lord Shiva. The temple was established in 734 A.D. The rulers of Mewar worshipped the idol in this double-storied, beautifully carved temple.

 The interiors of the temple are adorned with statues and carved pillars, while the steps on the outside touch the waters. The striking attraction here is the black marble idol of Shiva which is four-faced.

 Situated around 30 km from Eklingji Temple is the popular Nathdwara Temple, sitting on the banks of Banas. This pilgrimage destination has a rich aesthetic feel and an interesting story behind it. Visitors from all over flock to this temple to worship Lord Krishna.

 Walk around the streets of Nathdwara which is famous for its delicious sweets, 'pichhwai paintings' and ivory artifacts.

 Proceed to the hotel for overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Trip to Chittorgarh and Departure from Udaipur
itinerary.description:
Wake up to a refreshing morning and get ready for breakfast.

 On your last day of Udaipur package tour, explore the massive Chittorgarh Fort.

 Built by the Maurya rulers in the 7th Century, this fort has many stories of battles, sacrifice and romance to narrate. It is decked with palaces, temples and many towers including massive cenotaphs.

 Do not miss the Rana Kumbh's Palace, Fateh Prakash Palace, Meerabai Temple, Padmini's Palace and Jain temples which are situated in the fortress, which is also a UNESCO's world heritage site.

 Your Udaipur city tour package is incomplete without a visit to the fort of Chittorgarh, which symbolizes the gallant and pride of the Rajputs.

 Your eventful Udaipur city tour package comes to an end after visiting this gigantic fort which has many historical tales to narrate.

 We are positive that you are enchanted by the colours and the magic of Udaipur, making it the most memorable vacation.

 Check out from the hotel and proceed to the airport or railway station as per your arrangement.

itinerary.title: Udaipur
itinerary.description:
Welcome to Rajasthan, the royal city of India !!

  Upon arrival at Udaipur, the city of lakes, transfer to your hotel with a lake facing room in the old Udaipur city. This part of Udaipur city has its own charm as most of the heritage spots are located here.

  After check-in and relaxing a while, leave for city tour of Udaipur. Visit City Palace which is one of the most imposing heritage building of Udaipur, Jagdish Mandir, Saheliyon ki Badi, Vintage car museum,Doodh Talai, Karni Mata Temple and other places of tourist attractions in Udaipur.

  In the evening, enjoy a sunset boat ride on lake Pichola.

   Overnight stay at Udaipur

itinerary.title: Day trip of Haldighati, Kumbhalgarh and Ranakpur
itinerary.description:
After early breakfast, go for a full day trip to Haldighati, Kumbhalgarh and Ranakpur. 

 Your first destination in today’s sightseeing would be Haldighati , the famous battle field where the forces of Mewar under Maharana Pratap and the Mughal Army under Raja Man Singh had fought the battle of Haldighati in the year 1576.The famous horse Chetak of Maharana Pratap of Mewar had played a pivitol role in this battle and laid down its life to protect Maharana Pratap.There is a Museum and a cenotaph in rememberence of Chetak at Haldighati.

   After a visit to Haldighati, your next destination this day would be Kumbhalgarh fort. 

 A UNESCO world heritage site, the walls of Kumbhalgarh Fort extend over 38 Kms, being second only to the Great Wall of China.Kumbhalgarh fort was constructed by Rana Kumbha in the 15th Century AD and was the place where Maharana Pratap was born.

 There are over 360 Hindu and Jain Temples inside the Kumbhalgarh Fort. Lakhola Tank is yet another notable tourist point inside the fort.Apart from the fort, Kumbhalgarh is also famous for Kumbhalgarh wildlife sanctuary spread over 578 Sq. Kms. with a rich wildlife including wolf, leopards, sloth bear, hyena, jackal, jungle cat, sambhar, nilgai, chausingha.Safaris can be booked to enjoy a ride inside the wildlife sanctary.   After your visit to Kumbhalgarh fort, the next beautiful destination that one should not miss are the Jain temples of Ranakpur.

 The Jain Temples of Ranakpur, famous for their intricate carving on stone are dedicated to Trithankara Adinatha.The temples are an excellent example of Maru-Gurjara architecture. Temple is supported by 1444 marble pillars, carved in exquisite detail. The pillars are all differently carved  with exquisite detailing and no two pillars are the same.

  After spending time at this beautiful temple, return back to  Udaipur.Overnight stay at Udaipur.

itinerary.title: Day trip to Eklingji and Nathdwara
itinerary.description:
After  breakfast proceed to Eklingji Temple (about 22 Kms/1 Hr. Drive) , a famous temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, the ruling deity of Mewar Princely State. After darshan at Eklingji Temple,one may visit Sahastra Bahu temples of the early 10th century AD, dedicated to Lord Vishnu in Nagda just about 3 Kms from Eklingji Temple.

  Later, proceed to Nathdwara Temple located about 30 Kms further from Eklingji Temple.At Nathdwara, Hindu God Lord Krisna’s incarnation in the form of a 7 year old boy is worshiped. (Beware of local pandits who would pretend to take you for a VIP Darshan at the temple for some donations/money.Temple has no such VIP Darshan charges.).  Later return back to  Udaipur. Rest of time free for leisure or for shopping.

  Overnight stay at Udaipur.

itinerary.title: Trip to Chittorgarh and Departure from Udaipur
itinerary.description:
After breakfast, checkout from the hotel in time and proceed to your last but equally fascinating destination near Udaipur – Chittorgarh Fort.Another UNESCO’s world heritage site, Chittorgargh Fort is credited for being one of the largest forts of India.The fort is perched majestically over a 590 feet high hill and is adorned with 65 historic structures, among them 4 being palace complexes, 19 being temples, 4 being memorials and 20 functional water bodies.Vijay Stambha and Kirti Stambha are two of the most prominent landmarks of this fort.

 Other must see landmarks of this fort include  Rana Kumbha's palace , Fateh Prakash Palace, Gaumukh Reservoir, Padmini's Palace, Meerabai Temple Kalika Mata Temple, Tulja Bhawani Temple, Kshemankari Temple, Adbuthnath Temple and some Jain temples.

  Later transfer to Udaipur airport or raiway station on time as per your onward connection.

  End of a great tour from Waytoindia.com !!

itinerary.title: Arrival in Udaipur
itinerary.description:
Your Udaipur tour package commences with arrival at the airport or railway station. Transfers to the hotel in Udaipur will be arranged. Once you check in, relax and unwind in the cosy room at the hotel.The rest of the evening is at leisure. You can explore the surrounding areas of your hotel at your own will. 

 We recommend you to visit the Fateh Sagar Lake. Opt for paddle boating and bird watching here.The backdrop of the green mountains and the blue waters of this lake have given Udaipur the soubriquet of ‘the second Kashmir’. 

 You can also check out the local shopping arcades and boutique restaurants studded on the roadside. 

  Return back to the hotel for a good night’s sleep. 

  Popular Places for Shopping in Udaipur:  

    The Hathi Pol Bazaar: (open 09.30 a.m. to 05.30 p.m.). The Rajasthani minuscule paintings, Phad andPichwai paintings, colourful 'Nagra' slippers that are manufactured from camel skin are must buys from this marketplace.   The Bada Bazaar: (open 09.30 a.m. to 06.00 p.m.). This place provides a plethora of small shops and exhibitions that display brightly coloured traditional clothes and mojdis.     

    The Shilpgram: (open 11.00 a.m. to 07.00 p.m.). It is a heritage crafts village selling items like pottery, wooden furniture and handmade paintings.

itinerary.title: Udaipur City Tour
itinerary.description:
Much like the rest of Rajasthan, Udaipur too houses some of the most glorious palaces and forts in the world and each one of them is worth a visit. Start your day really early with a healthy breakfast and head for a Udaipur city tour on the second day of this Udaipur tour package for couples. 

 Later, return back to the hotel for an overnight stay.  Top Attractions in Udaipur to visit on this Udaipur tour package for couples: 

 ·         The City Palace: (open 09.30 a.m. to 04.30 p.m.; entry fees is INR 50 / 300 for Indians / foreigners). This palace is a conglomeration of several structures with towers, galleries and cupolas. See the crystal gallery at the palace that houses world's largest private collection of crystal.
