 ·         Saheliyon Ki Bari: (open 08.00 a.m. to 07.00 p.m.; entry fees is INR 5 / 100 for Indians / foreigners).This garden was built for the group of 48 young women attendants who travelled along with the princess to Udaipur as part of her dowry. Behold the lush greenery, fountains, flowers, lotus pools and marble artefacts here. 

 ·         The Jagdish Temple:(open05:00 a.m. to 10:30 p.m.; no entry fees). This temple is one of the oldest in India. Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the idol is carved out of a single piece of black stone.

itinerary.title: Udaipur –Kumbhalgarh (110 kms / 2 hours 30 minutes approximately)
itinerary.description:
On the third day of your Udaipur tour package for couples, you will check out from the hotel in Udaipur and drive to Kumbhalgarh. 

 On arrival, check in at the hotel and head to visit the Mewar fortress- the Kumbhalgarh fort (open 09.00 a.m. to 05.00 p.m.; entry fees is INR 10 / 100 for Indians / foreigners).After the Great Wall of China, this citadel has the second largest wall in the world.Splendid views and a sneak peek into the Aravalli countryside await you here. Adventures like horse riding and camping can also be enjoyed here. 

 Return back home for a comfortable overnight stay with your better half. 

 Other Things to Do in Kumbhalgarh on this Udaipur tour package for couples: 

    Trekking: Exciting trails from Kumbhalgarh to Ranakpur, Kumbhalgarh to Ghanerao and Kumbhalgarh to Thandiberi can be undertaken. The roads are not so rigid and these trekking expeditions can be opted by people of all age groups.    

    Horse Safari: This can be undertaken inside the Kumbhalgarh wildlife sanctuary. It offers a thrilling experience of the animals and the natural surroundings of the wildlife sanctuary. Birds including Red Spur Owls, Grey Jungle Fowl, Doves, White Breasted Kingfisher and Golden Oriole can be seen here.     

    Attend the Gher Ghoomer festival: Dance to the captivating tunes of the huge drums at this traditional festival of the local tribes of Kumbhalgarh. Throw colours and splashing water and enjoy local dishes at this festival.

itinerary.title: Kumbhalgarh – Chittorgarh(160 kms / 3 hours 30 minutes approximately)
itinerary.description:
On this fourth day of your Udaipur tour package for couples, check out from the hotel in Kumbhalgarh and drive down to Chittorgarh. On arrival, check in at the hotel and head to see the grand Chittorgarh fort(open 09.00 a.m. to 05.00 p.m.; entry fees is INR 10 / 100 for Indians / foreigners). The fort is humongous with 22 water bodies, towers, palaces and temples spanning over an area of 700 acres. 

 Later, return back to the hotel to spend the night with your partner in the comfort of the hotel. 

 Best Restaurants in Chittorgarh to Eat at on this Udaipur tour package for couples: 

    The Padmini Restaurant: (open11:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m.). Indian, Continental, Chinese and traditional Rajasthani cuisines are served here. It is a part of the Hotel Padmini which is spread lavishly across 20 acres with breathtaking landscape.    

    The Golden Bar and Restaurant: (open08:00 a.m. to 11:00 p.m.). It is one of the modest hotels in Chittorgarh. Beautiful, clean and a friendly staff make this hotel a must visit. The best dishes include black dal served with butter parathas and gatte ki kichdi.    

 The Zero Degree Restro and Lounge: (open11:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m.)This place is a well-renowned rooftop dining place in Chittorgarh. With great views of the Vijay Stambh, enjoy Rajasthanikadi and international cuisines here.

itinerary.title: Drop at Airport / Railway Station and Departure
itinerary.description: Your Udaipur tour package for couples has come to an unfortunate halt here. After servicing your stomach with healthy breakfast, transfer to the airport or railway station will be arranged and you will start your onward journey to home. We hope you enjoyed thoroughly and will let us serve you again.

itinerary.title: Delhi – Lansdowne (270 km. / 7 hr.)
itinerary.description:
The tour begins as we receive you at the Delhi Airport or railway station. Get started with the journey as we drive you to the first destination Lansdowne, a quaint hill station originally developed by the British. Check in at the hotel and relax.

  For the remaining part of the day, you are free to savour the overwhelming beauty of blue pines and oaks all around. Pollution-free environment and rejuvenating weather of the place shall mark a great beginning of the tour.

itinerary.title: Lansdowne Sightseeing
itinerary.description:
Be ready for a heart-warming exploration of the region after breakfast.

  Visit the top attractions like Bulla Tal Lake, Tarkeshwar Mahadev Temple and the Churches of St. John’s and St. Mary’s. Christian Cemetery and Tiger Cave are other highlights of the tour. Later visit the 19th century Garhwali Mess, known for its splendid architecture and heritage value.

  You will also be visiting the Garhwal Rifles War Memorial dedicated to the regiment which participated in each of the two World Wars.  Join an enjoyable walk to the Tiffin Top (Tip-n-Top), a vantage point offering breathtaking views of the Himalayan peaks. Few parts of Tibet are also visible from the spot.  Save your appreciation for the astonishing Bhim Pakora, a pile of two self-balancing rocks that weigh several thousand kg. It is possible to move the upper rock even with a finger, but impossible to bring it down. Take a small excursion to the Karnva Ashram, associated with the great legend of Shakuntala and Bharat, the characters of Kalidasa’s eternal literature. Return to the hotel late evening.

itinerary.title: Lansdowne – Ranikhet (218 km. / 6 hr.)
itinerary.description:
Have your breakfast early morning and leave for Ranikhet, another spectacular hill station nestled in the Himalayas. 

 Transfer to the hotel and relax before you are escorted for the full day sightseeing tour. 

 You will be visiting the attractions like Chaubatia Gardens and Orchards, Jhula Devi Temple, Kataramal Sun Temple, Haidakhan Temple, KRC Museum, Golf Course Syalikhet Village and Bhalu Dam.

itinerary.title: Day Excursion to Almora (48 km. / 2 hr.)
itinerary.description:
After you enjoy the breakfast, get ready for a memorable day trip to Almora, a charming hill station and cultural hub of the region. Resting amidst the luxuriantly green forests and witnessing the journey of serpentine rivers, the place exemplifies nature’s uncompromised facet. Admire the mesmerizing views of the snow-covered Himalayas and enjoy the visual treat of sprawling valleys.

  Visit popular places like Deer Park, Chitai Temple, Bright End Corner, Jageshwar Temple, Kasar Devi Temple, Lal Bazaar and others.  If you are interested, you can plan an unforgettable trek from Almora to Jageshwar in the months from October to March. The low-altitude trek is great even for the beginners and passes through a variety of landscapes, ranging from green fields and forests and some beautiful villages. 

 It is a fabulous exploration through the paths adorned with rhododendrons and deodars. Highlights of the trek also include the visits to Jageshwar Temple and Kasar Devi Temple. 

  Return to the hotel at Ranikhet by evening and enjoy your dinner before going to sleep.

itinerary.title: Ranikhet – Nainital (55 km. / 2 hr.)
itinerary.description:
Today, you will be driven to one of the best honeymoon locations in India, the city of Nainital. 

 Finish your breakfast and enjoy the scenic drive to the hill station. As you arrive at Nainital, check in at the hotel and relax. The remaining part of the day is for you to spend at leisure. Some possible activities would be to take leisurely stroll along the Naini Lake which lends its name to the place; visit the shops at the Mall Road and admire the unbelievable sight of the entire area lit up with lights at night. 

 Get back to your hotel late evening and enjoy your dinner.

itinerary.title: Nainital Sightseeing
itinerary.description: Begin the day with a healthy breakfast and leave the hotel for the full day sightseeing. You may visit the pear-shaped lake and enjoy boating while witnessing the outstanding natural magnificence. Other worth-visiting attractions in the city include Naina Devi Temple, Nainital Zoo, St. John’s Church and Eco Cave Garden. Don’t miss the aerial ropeway adventure. Retire to your hotel at the end and enjoy dinner.

itinerary.title: Nainital – Jim Corbett (145 km. / 4 hr.)
itinerary.description:
Check out from the hotel after having lunch and get ready for visiting Jim Corbett National Park, one of the oldest wildlife reserves in Asia.

  On the route, you will be visiting the places like Bhimtal, Naukuchiatal and Sattal, each famous for its splendid lakes and scenic beauty. Continue the journey to Corbett and check in at the forest resort or lodge on arrival. 

 Feel the thrill of staying close to the wild, well-known for being abode to the royal Bengal tigers. You may choose an optional jeep safari ride through the park after lunch.

itinerary.title: Corbett Tiger Safari
itinerary.description:
Begin the thrilling day with breakfast and get prepared for a tiger jeep safari. You have the great chances to cross paths with the undisputed king of the jungle, the tiger. 

 Watch other wild animals before getting back for breakfast. You may opt for the elephant safari ride after lunch for another tryst with the wild. Enjoy your dinner at the end of the day.

itinerary.title: Dhikala – Delhi (255 km. / 6 hr.)
itinerary.description:
Enjoy the final tiger safari trip to the forest before breakfast.

  Get back to the resort or lodge and have your breakfast. Pack your bags and check out for the final journey which takes you back to Delhi.

 The tour ends here...

itinerary.title: Delhi - Mussorie
itinerary.description: Early morning drive to Mussorie, Enroute visit Haridwar covering temples of Mata Mansa Devi and Chamunda Devi. On arrival, check into the hotel. Rest of the day free at leisure. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay at Mussoorie.

itinerary.title: Mussorie
itinerary.description: After breakfast in the morning visit the most beautiful 'Kempty fall, Gun hill and company garden. Explore the Mall road in the evening and return back to hotel for dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Mussorie - Ranikhet
itinerary.description: After breakfast in the hotel, drive to Ranikhet. On arrival check into the hotel and rest of the day free at leisure. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay at Ranikhet.

itinerary.title: Ranikhet
itinerary.description: After a healthy breakfast proceed for full day sightseeing tour of Ranikhet and nearby places, Explore Hedhakhan Temple, visit Chaukhotia Apple orchid as well as golf course and return back to hotel for dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Ranikhet - Almora
itinerary.description: After breakfast check out on time and drive to another beautiful hill station, Almora. Arrive and transfer to the hotel and rest of the day is at leisure to explore the unspoiled flora & fauna of this beautiful hill station. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay in Almora.

itinerary.title: Almora
itinerary.description: After enjoying breakfast, a full day excursion to Binsar Wildlife sanctuary a 30 min. drive from Almora, Explore all other important places nearby Almora. Return back to hotel for dinner & overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Almora - Nainital
itinerary.description: In morning after breakfast on time drive to Nainital, the most beautiful hill station of Kumaon. On arrival check into the hotel and rest of the day free at your own leisure. Dinner & Overnight stay in Nainital.

itinerary.title: Nainital
itinerary.description: After enjoying breakfast in the hotel proceed for full day sightseen of Nainital. Enjoy the boat ride at Naini Lake, followed by lake tour and visit all other important places in and around Nainital. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Nainital - Delhi
itinerary.description: After breakfast in the hotel on time, drive to Delhi. On arrival, transfer to Airport/Hotel for onward journey.   Tour and Services Ends.

itinerary.title: JammuArrival – Katra (50 km.)
itinerary.description: Take the scheduled flight or train to reach Jammu, the winter capitalof the state. Our representative will meet you at the airport or railwaystation and escort you to Katra, the base from where the trek to the shrinebegins. On arrival, transfer to the hotel and take rest. Move out in theevening to explore the town and its markets full of souvenirs. Return to thehotel late evening.

itinerary.title: Katra – Vaishno Devi Shrine – Katra
itinerary.description: Depending on the option you choose, you will: Begin the trek of about 13 km. early morning to access the shrine. On the way, you may wish to visit many holy spots like Banganga Temple, Charan Paduka Temple, Adi Kumari Shrine and others. Pray at the main shrine and get back to Katra by evening. Board the helicopter to reach the helipad at Sanjhi Chat, from where you will trek a small distance to the shrine. After visiting the shrine, get back to Sanjhi Chat and board the helicopter to Katra.  Return to the hotel and take rest.

itinerary.title: Katra – Jammu
itinerary.description: Have breakfast and check out to leave for Jammu. We will drop youat the airport or railway station for your preferred connecting journey. Thetour ends here.

itinerary.title: Arrive at Govind ghat(1828 m.)
itinerary.description:
Your Valley of flowers trek starts from Govindghat

Arrive at Govindghat and have an overnight stay at the hotel. Govindghat is located in Chamoli district of Uttarakhand, India. Jolly Grant Airport in Dehradun is the nearest airport to Govindghat which is situated at a distance of approximately 292 km. The nearest railway station to Govind ghat is Rishikesh on NH 58 at a height of around 5,500 feet. Govindghat is visited by many pilgrims on Chardham Yatra, so it is a major place for the accommodation of the pilgrims. The place offers natural beauty and escapes from chaotic urban life.

itinerary.title: Trek from Govindghat to Ghangariya(3048 m., 14 km.)
itinerary.description:
We shall start the second day of your valley of flowers trek by beginning our trek from Govindghat to Ghangariya.

 The distance between these places is about 14 km. which takes around 5 to 6 hours to reach.The uphill trek begins with steep valleys. The steep valleys last for first 3 km. of the trek and again come towards the last 3 km. 

 If you are not able to cope up with the trek, then you can take a pony or a helicopter. Ponies cost around Rs. 800 to Rs. 2,000. Helicopters are available only during the clear weather. 

 The best time to undertake Valley of flowers trek is June to September i.e. the monsoon season. The helicopter facility is not available during that period. Palanquins are also available for elderly people which are relatively costlier and costs around Rs. 8000 per person.  On the way, you come across Pulma and Bhyuandar, both of these villages were heavily damaged during 2013 floods in Uttarakhand but are now rehabilitated.  After reaching Ghangariya, there is an overnight stay before we begin our next day of Valley of flowers trek.

itinerary.title: Trek from Ghangariya to Valley of Flowers(10 km.)
itinerary.description:
Day 3 of Valley of flowers trek starts with early morning breakfast.

Trek to Valley of Flowers about 10 km. which takes about 6 to 7 hours to reach.  The 50 percent of trek towards the valley of flowers is steep uphill while the remaining part is not so steep. The landscapes of the valley are a feast to eyes. You come across vibrant flowers, beautiful mountain ranges, many species of birds, insects, butterflies, etc.  The sight of thousands of flowers blooming astonishes trekkers. Many streams flow through the valley which further adds to the soulful beauty of nature. On the right side of the valley is the snowy Ratban Parvat (6,125m.) and on the left side is the Nar Parvat. Also, relish the beautiful mountain ranges of Nilgiri Parvat (6,479m.) and Ghori Parbat (6,950m.). Head back to the camp/hotel for overnight stay at Ghangariya.

itinerary.title: Trek from Ghangariya to Kunthkhal Top(3700 m.-4430 m., 9 km.)
itinerary.description: After early morning breakfast begins the trek from Ghangariya to Kunth khal Top which is approximately 3,700 m. to 4,430 m. The total distance of the trek is about 9km. The route is moderately easy and not much steep valleys come along the way. From the Kunthkhal top, the trekkers get an incredible view of the valley. The snow-capped mountains and the vegetation with a cold windy breeze are lifetime time experience. The way to Kunthkhal top diverts from a point named BamniDhaur. The trek route will introduce you to rivers, streams, and glaciers. The trek route is a visual treat as a lot of flora and fauna can be seen in this scenic place. Spend some time on the Kunthkhal top and if you are physically fit enough you can take a tour until the end of the valley at Tipra Khark. Later head back to Ghangariya for an overnight stay in the hotel.

itinerary.title: Early Morning Trek to Govind ghat
itinerary.description:
After morning breakfast, this 5th day of your Valley of flowers trek, head back to Govind ghat. 

 It is purely a downhill trek as opposed to the day 2 trek where it was uphill. You also have the option of taking ponies. After reaching Auli, the vehicle shall take you to Chairlift Point where you have to climb the stairs. From the chairlift point, you shall take the chairlift up to the tower number 10 where you can indulge in adventure activities. You can drive mountain scooters; visit the artificial lake and much more. You can visit the highest peak of Uttrakhand, Nanda Devi from where you can enjoy the magnificent view. At the day end, you have to come to the parking bay by chairlift and drive back to Joshimath to head towards the hotel for an overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Check Out
itinerary.description: Your Valley of Flowers Trek ends here. After having breakfast, check out from the hotel and leave for your onward destination.

itinerary.title: Delhi – Haridwar (200 kms/ 5 Hours Drive Approximately)
itinerary.description:
Your valley of flowers Uttarakhand packagestarts with arrival in Delhi. As soon as you arrive, you will be escorted to Haridwar by road. Let the eagerness build up, as you travel through the hilly roads.People from all over India visit this spiritual place and take some divine dips into the holy river Ganga.

 On arrival in Haridwar, quickly check in to the hotel and freshen up as today you will be exploring one of the seven most divine places. First, you will be visiting Haridwar’s holy Ghat which is popularly known as Har Ki Paudi. It is a sacred bathing ghat in Haridwar where mystical events take place.  This ghat masses the marvellous Kumbh Mela, during which many people have a bath in River Ganga.The place houses the foot imprints of Lord Vishnu on one of the stones of the ghat.

  Later you can walk around Har Ki Paudi’s elegant markets and put your bargaining skills at work. In the evening, be present for the rituals thatwill be performed by the priests on the banks ofRiver Ganga. Here you will have adelightful and sanctified experience with the graceful lamps floating into the water. In addition to this, the sounds of echoing mantras will fill the atmosphere which will have your mind calm down from worldly matters and let you get engrossed in the flow of chants. After tasting this divine beauty, you will head back to the hotel for overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Haridwar – Joshimath (300 kms / 8 Hours Drive Approximately)
itinerary.description:
After having some aromatic breakfast on the second day of your valley of flowers Uttarakhand package, head to Joshimath – one of the most sacred towns.Snuggled with love in the Chamoli district of Uttarakhand, this celestial place is admired by the Hindus for being a vital pilgrimage centre of the country. 

  En-route, halt at the holy sites of Rudraprayag, Devprayag and Karnprayag.

  Named after Lord Shiva, Rudraprayag lies at the convergence of two rivers, namely Alaknanda and Mandakini. The small town will bless your eyes with majestic views. Visit Kedarnath temple if time permits.

  Devprayag is set amidst the rolling Himalayan ranges and Ganga technically flows from here when rivers Bhagirathi and Alaknanda meet to form the sacred river.

  Karnprayag is home to Uma Devi Temple along with lush green hills at the background and a foaming white river.

  After sinking in the beauty of these places, head to the hotel in Joshimath and spend the overnight there.

itinerary.title: Joshimath - Govindghat – Ghangaria(20kms/ 1 Hour Drive Approximately)
itinerary.description:
Get yourself ready for an eventful day on the third day of this valley of flowers Uttarakhand package. You will need your energy so have a stomach filling breakfast and head to the small majestic village of Govidghat. It is a beautiful hamlet which is popular among pilgrims and also adventure enthusiasts as it is the starting point of the trek to valley of flowers. Start trekking to the beautiful Ghangaria village which is 14 kms from Govidghat and 10,200 feet above the sea level. It is the last human habitation centre you will come across on this trek.

  The valley of flowers trek difficulty is from easy to medium and hence can be undertaken by one and all. Once you reach the base camp in Ghangaria, check -in at the hotel and relax for the evening.

  Spend the overnight after savouring a lip-smacking dinner meal.

itinerary.title: Ghangaria - Hemkund Sahib – Ghangaria(5 kms trek)
itinerary.description:
On the fourth day of the valley of flowers Uttarakhand package tour, have breakfast and regain your energy for the trek to Hemkund Sahib.

  It is a highly revered place of worship for the Sikh community and the dizzying height of 4636 m is what makes it even more popular. On your way to the gurudwara, the attractive views of glorious Himalayas, cascading waterfalls and verdant dense forests will leave you bewitched.

 On finally reaching atop, take in the view of the only pentagonal gurudwara in the world. The Hemkund Sahib was once a meditational ground of Lord Laxman as per the devotees and you must take a dip in its holy waters.

 Return back to the hotel in Ghangaria and spend the overnight in the comfort of the cosy room.

itinerary.title: Ghangaria - Valley of flowers – Ghangaria (3 kms trek)
itinerary.description:
Valley of flowers is a secret jewel situated at an elevation of 3250 meters. The valley is a vibrant and splendid national park and is a dwelling to several rare and scarce varieties of animals like Asiatic black bear, snow leopard, blue sheep, etc. The trek is known for its mind-blowing meadows of endemic alpine flowers and the diversity of flora.  It covers an area up to 87 sq. km. and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

 Post breakfast at the base camp, head to explore this wonderland that lures thousands of nature lovers, avid photographers and adventure junkies every year.

 It is also popular among pilgrims as this is the place where Lord Hanuman obtained the sanjeevani herbs for Lord Laxman.

 Return back to Ghangaria base camp in the evening for an overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Ghangaria - Govindghat – Rudraprayag(130kms/ 4 Hours Drive Approximately)
itinerary.description:
On this day of your valley of flowers Uttarakhand package, have an early morning breakfast and go on board to trek and descend down back to Govindghat. On reaching the quaint village, transfers to Rudraprayag will be arranged for. Treat your eyes to the splendid views as you drive from Govindghat to Rudraprayag. On arrival, check in at the hotel and relax for the rest of the day.

  If you wish, you can visit the Koteshwar Mahadev Temple (open 6 a.m. to 5 p.m., no entry fees). It is set on the banks of the Alaknanda River and the temple is in the form of a cave. Devotees believe that the idols here are formed naturally after Lord Shiva meditated in the cave on his way to Kedarnath.

  Taking back this once in a lifetime experience, you head back to the hotel for a comfortable overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Rudraprayag - Rishikesh – Haridwar(160kms/ 5 Hours Drive Approximately)
itinerary.description:
The seventh day of your valley of flowers Uttarakhand package commences with a delicious breakfast, post which you will embark on a journey to Haridwar via Rishikesh.

  Your journey to Haridwar is incomplete without catching on all sightseeing spots in Rishikesh. Cross the Ram Jhula and Laxman Jhula, two iron suspension bridges laid across the river Ganges. The gushing water running below while you cross the bridge will give you a thriving experience.

  Next, head to Neelkanth Mahadev Temple (open 6 a.m. to 7 p.m., no entry fees). History states that Lord Shiva drank the poison tossed from the oceans which turned his neck blue. The temple is situated on the same location where this event took place.

  Continue the journey and once you reach Haridwar, check in at the hotel and relax.

  In the evening, visit the Chilla Wildlife Sanctuary (open6:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m.; entry fees INR 150 / 600 for Indians / foreigners). Ithouses tigers, small cats, elephants, bears and also various migratory birds can be spotted here.

  Return back to the hotel and spend the overnight comfortably.

itinerary.title: Haridwar- Delhi for Departure(200 kms / 5 Hours Drive Approximately)
itinerary.description: Finishing your last breakfast on this valley of flowers Uttarakhand package, you head back to the airport.You wish for the days to never end, but unfortunately your vacation tour comes to a halt here. With all the memories and heart filling experiences, you head to the Delhi airport to catch a flight back home.

itinerary.title: Delhi - Varanasi
itinerary.description:
This morning, pickup from the hotel and transfer to Airport to board the flight to Varanasi.

 On arrival, meet and assist by our representative and transfer to hotel.

 Later proceed for a city tour of Varanasi covering some famous temples in Varanasi like Durga temple, Vishwanath temple and Sankat Mochan temple.

 In the evening enjoy the boat cruise on the Ganges. Watch Hindu devotees performing the evening "Aarti Puja with lighted lamps.

 Return back to hotel in the evening for dinner & Overnight in Varanasi.

itinerary.title: Varanasi [Sarnath & Banaras Hindu University]
itinerary.description:
After enjoying breakfast in the hotel proceed to Sarnath.

 One of the holiest Buddhist sites in the world, Sarnath is where Buddha preached his first Sermon in 590 B.C.

 Also visit Sarnath Museum - An excellent collection of Buddhist art and sculptures found at the site.

Later return back to Varanasi to visit one of the oldest Universities in the world, the Banaras Hindu University. Also visit to Bharat Mata Temple.

 Return back to hotel in the evening. Enjoy dinner & Overnight stay at hotel in Varanasi.

itinerary.title: Varanasi - Delhi
itinerary.description:
After a leisurely breakfast in the hotel, visit the markets famous for Banaras Silks.

 In the afternoon transfer to Airport to connect the flight to Delhi.

 On arrival at Delhi, transfer to hotel/International Airport to board the flight for onward destination.
 
End of tour and our Services.

itinerary.title: Arrive Delhi
itinerary.description: Welcome at New Delhi International airport. On arrival the guest will be meet by our representative with the traditional Indian welcome, and transfered to the hotel. Overnight stay at Hotel.

itinerary.title: Delhi – Agra [220 kms/4 hrs]
itinerary.description: Morning After breakfast half day city tour of Old & New Delhi. In afternoon drive to Agra. On arrival check into the hotel. Dinner & Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Agra – Bharatpur [70 kms/1.5 hrs]
itinerary.description: After enjoying breakfast visit Taj Mahal & Agra Fort. Then drive to Bharatpur Bird Sanctuary. On arrival check into the hotel. Rest of the day free to explore the Sanctuary. Overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Bharatpur Bird Sanctuary
itinerary.description: Morning and afternoon walk to the Sanctuary to best view of the migratory birds. In the afternoon enjoy a rickshaw ride. Evening at leisure and overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Bharatpur – Sariska [180 kms/3 hrs]
itinerary.description: After breakfast & leisure drive to Sariska National Park, on arrival check into the hotel. Enjoy the dinner & overnight stay at hotel.

itinerary.title: Sariska National Park
itinerary.description: Morning and afternoon, an exciting Jeep drive to the Sariska Sanctuary to view wildlife. It is the home to an exotic variety of wild animals and beautiful birds. Here, herds of golden deer dart and pirouette gracefully, playing hideand seek with their shadow, and the big wild cat, the tiger, roams free. Overnight stay at Sariska.

itinerary.title: Sariska – Ranthambore [240 kms/5 hrs]
itinerary.description: After an early breakfast drive to Ranthambore. On arrival in Ranthambore, check into the wildlife Resort. In the afternoon Jeep Safari to the National Park. Enjoy dinner at Resort & overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Ranthambore
itinerary.description: Morning and evening Safari in open trucks and jeeps to Ranthambore National Park and spend time tracking Tigers and viewing other wildlife attractions of the park. Back to Resort till evening. Enjoy dinner and overnight at the Resort.

itinerary.title: Ranthambore – Jaipur [180 kms/3 hrs]
itinerary.description: Afterenjoying breakfast at Resort drive to Jaipur. On arrival in Jaipur check intothe hotel. In the afternoon visit city sightseeing tour visit including City Palace, Hawa Mahal, Jantar Mantar & Colour market etc.   Inevening visit Choki Dhani the cultural of Rajasthan with an India tradition. Enjoy the dinner at choki Dhani and back to the hotel till late evening. Overnight stay at Hotel.

itinerary.title: Jaipur – Delhi [265 kms/6 hrs]
itinerary.description: After an early breakfast an excursion tour to Amber Fort. In Afternoon drive back to Delhi. On arrival transfer to the airport to board the flight for onward destination.
